review_text: ONE OF THE WORST PROFESSORS THAT I HAVE EVER COME ACROSS HE DOES NOT HELP ANY STUDENT  HE NEVER RESPONDS TO EMAIL WORST GRADING SHAMELESS PROFESSOR

One of my favorite profs. I came in with no knowledge of databases and was able to get caught up with the class rather easy. First lecture that has been worth showing up to since Covid. Homework assignments can be rather tricky but not the end of the world. Midterm and Final didn't contain trick questions and were graded fairly.

Amazing professor. CSE 512 is a light course. If you already have a knowledge of databases, its gonna be easy. Good assignments and good lecture notes. A few tricky coding assignments, but overall managable. Mid-exam was descriptive and final was MCQ. Would recommend if you are looking for a great in-person lecture experience and an easy A

He makes the class pretty easy and clear to understand, but he feels like he's unprepared for most of his lectures. If you ask him a question he'll often misinterpret it or brush it off. Grading is lenient and the homework is interesting, but they're also pretty cookie cutter so you'll only touch the surface level of some of the tech.

Prof very nice and caring, hates cheaters tho. Lectures not that complicated, but a lot to cram for midterm/final via basically rote learning, very doable to do good on the exams. Projects some were really easy, some decently hard with only a week to do, the hard one will suck but its downhill from there. One of the easiest 500's with a great prof!
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Caring

review_text: He's a really nice guy but just didn't feel like he was prepared. He cares that everyone gets a good grade, but it also feels like you're not learning anything too complicated. The content overlaps a lot with CSE412 (except for the basic SQL part) with some extra information sprinkled in for the distributed part. It's not rigorous at all.

I took his class in Spring 2021 during covid when fully online. He tried to really help at the end of the semester to make it less stressful for us. He recorded lectures but did post them a week later and wasn't available out of class much. I learned a lot in this class and thought it was great as an elective. I would recommend!

He's a nice guy, just disorganized. During Covid he uploaded lectures days after. Students had to keep emailing TAs to clarify vague assignment directions, and shared their responses on Discord. The one time I emailed the professor, my email tracker said he read it 2 hours later, but I never got a response; not sure if that's a norm for him.

I don't know why this professor is ranked so highly! The class is the easiest I have ever taken. Professor teaches basic things that even an undergrad student will know! Cakewalk, easy to get A, A+. Please don't take it as it is not a graduate-level course.

Tries to scare you off with the rules in his first class. You would learn a lot from this class. This professor is an ocean of knowledge. Not the toughest grading but you can lose points if you are not careful with the specific grading criteria and plagiarism.
Very interactive class and communicated a lot with the class. Cared how we were doing and is flexible.

Very interactive classes and interesting lectures with real life examples. clear grading. Subject is very useful for jobs and gives hands on experience on latest things. Only thing i did not like was the assignments and projects are same from several semesters and they need to be changed
Great course. You will learn a lot of details about engineering distributed data systems. The only drawback is that exams can be difficult.
Enjoyed the class. The assignments and group project are really great and gives you hands-on experience. The midterm was difficult but the grading was not too tough. Highly recommended.

He is a pretty chill professor. The lectures are okay. Definitely need to attend and participate in all lectures to do well on the tests. This time, midterms and finals were both difficult. Assignments are doable and really awesome. The project also gives you hands-on experience in Apache Spark and AWS.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

review_text: This class is a must if you plan to pursue a career in data science. Dr. Sarwat is definitely an expert in database systems and that shows in the way he runs discussions in class. The class is never boring since the prof shows how to do database stuff using the PostgreSQL psql interface. The final exam was a bit difficult but not too bad.

Good prof. lectures are clear and in-class activities are engaging. There are 5 prog. assignments and they are not difficult. The prof focus on the practice side of DDS, which is very useful when you apply for jobs. Having a 3 hrs class on a Friday afternoon is not cool though.

He's a cool guy, his lectures are pretty fun, and the homework was clearly laid out. But he created a lot of unnecessary stress with his extra grading policies. For example, it said on the syllabus that if you scored less than 50% on the final you would fail the class. He later relaxed the policies so they were just scare tactics I guess.

This class seems difficult at the beginning but the prof makes it easy. Lecturing style is good and easy to follow. HWs require programming with Python, PostgreSQL, and MongoDB. The project gave us hands-on experience with Hadoop and Spark. The pace of the class can sometimes be slow but thats because Prof tries to clear all doubts in class.
nice, knowledgeable and good lecturer. makes the content very practical. HWs and project are easy but exams are difficult. Attending the class is necessary to get a good participation grade.

Great professor - very nice and smart. The fun part though is when he runs interative lab sessions using PostgreSQL in class. He shows us step-by-step how to write SQL commands. I wish more professors would do the same.

He's very nice, knowledgable, and smart, but that doesn't mean he's a good teacher. His homework and project assignments are poorly explained, and grading is tough. He also has difficulty explaining new concepts: he skips through lectures because he thinks everyone is as quick to learn as he is. Interesting dude, but not for a graded course.

Could have been a great teacher but already 2 weeks in to the Semester, already can't get a reply from him through email after emailing him twice. He's too busy with his twitter presence to even check his emails. Apart from that could have been a good teacher.

Friendly professor. Good lectures. Cheat sheet is allowed on exams, though won't help much. Assignments were very easy, and they are 25% of your grade. Project is okay (code on Scala, so you have to learn a new language), although 10 pages report at the end is not cool.

Among the best classes I took at ASU. The material is very practical and the professor shows enthusiasm teaching it. A lot of interactive demos using PostgreSQL in class, which helps us get a better feel of the abstract concepts. Assignment are easy but the exams are a bit challenging. Overall, a very good class!

ASU should have more professors like Dr. Sarwat. He knows the material very well and shows interest in teaching it. He also cares a lot about the success of his students. The lectures are great and he incorporates a lot of activities / discussions. For improvement, I think the group project could have been a bit more challenging.

A good professor. He knows what he's teaching and will make sure to answer all questions in class. The best thing about this class is the project in which we learn how to run database queries in Spark. Highly recommended!

Sarwat is a good prof. Lots of assignments and project work but you will learn a lot. Midterm was a bit difficult but the final was easy. You will need to attend the lecture or otherwise you may not be able to follow. I would have liked it if the prof covered topics related to big data.

Seemed like he was going to be fine for the first half of the semester. Towards the end he got extremely disorganized and was very unclear when teaching more complicated material. Never gave feedback for why things were wrong. Had multiple assignments where the solutions were incorrect. Spent 30% of lecture talking about random stuff. Bad Professor

Dr. Sarwat is one of the best computer science professors at ASU. He has extreme knowledge on the database and SQL subject and is very patient answering questions during class. He sometimes relates the abstract concepts to current tech news, which makes it easy for us to understand. I totally recommend the class - a lot to learn!
Course Project and assignments are nice. Classes are just ok, nothing extra-ordinary.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--GROUP PROJECTS

review_text: Clear lectures. Useful assignments. Project is highly related to cutting edge computing technology.

Prof. Sarwat is an excellent professor. His course schedule is very clear and there will not be many changes. You will learn many new and hot technologies as a part of his course that add value  to your resume. The course load is very balanced. I would definitely recommend his course.  Attendance and class participation is important.

Course projects and assignments related to latest technologies such as Apache Spark, Hadoop, MongoDB etc. which is good to learn and put on resume. There is only one midterm exam which is easy and no finals.

not so hard grading but definitely not a good prof. u will learn nothing from himattending class. attendance is kind of mandatory since he takes meaningless quizzes now and then. however, projectsassignments  r good but group sizes r 2 much. Avoid if u want to learn!!

The best professor I have seen ever. He had immense knowledge in his field. He scares u on the first day saying subject is very tough. but he makes course a free walk. We had group assignments too. One thing is sure, course project will be your top listing in linkedin profile. Professor makes you work on latest technologies for project .

His 310 class is pretty straightforward, and the homework wasn't difficult since it's just practice for what you learned in the lecture. There were two Midterms, and the lower grade gets dropped. The final wasn't too difficult as well. The two projects could be done on your own or with a partner. Review the slides and you'll do fine.

Professor Yang is a great choice for 310! Homework and projects are on topic while still relatively challenging. Lectures are informative although they could be a little slow at times, but there's good insight into real-world application throughout. Tests can be a little tricky; use your whole cheat sheet. TAs are also very helpful in this class!

He makes CSE 310 feel so much easier than it is. Homework is super light, with basically 2 questions per week. He's a super chill and cool guy and his lectures are good. Just make sure to study the lecture notes and do good on the tests (which have cheat sheets) and you will be completely fine.

Dr. Yang my goat idk course might be a bit different in the future sometimes his lectures are unbelievably dry (looking at u RB trees) exams are important so def study hard for those but otherwise the course isn't too hard, i think it's a good balance of relatively easy but very thought-provoking assignments

Not much to say about 310, but prof YZ makes it fairly easy for everyone. Two midterms but the one with higher grade is taken, and then a final. Also two projects. Don't know about future classes, but it was pretty chill. He's also chill and his lectures (neither recitation) are required. Just don't forget to study.
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

review_text: Professor makes the lectures enjoyable and fun to attend. Explains concepts very well. There were 8 homework assignments Homework is a little tough at times but not too difficult, everything you do seems to be related to the material you learn and doing the homework does help on exams. We got one cheat sheet on the midterm and 2 on the final.

Perception In Robotics. The professor is a nice person and cares about students. However, the lectures were bad. Apart from camera geometry nothing else was explained well. We chose between 6 projects based on his research. We did not use any course material in our project but it was a very interesting one! We did not learn/use ROS.

Perception In Robotics. This course was mainly graded by the final project. The professor was a very nice person, projects are guided by a PhD students. He is very knowledgeable and explains concepts multiple times if needed.Grades depend on the final presentation scored by PhDs, so even if you work really hard, you might get an A instead of an A+.
Hilarious professor, because he's young and gets along with the students. His teaching is very engaging. Projects make you do a lot but are a lot of fun.

He is a really good professor and cares about what he does. This was my favorite class this semester. The textbook is really helpful for keeping up with the content and understanding it better. I do recommend starting on the coding assignments earlier rather than later as they can be challenging. He really cares about the students succeeding.

Professor Yang made data structures and algorithm fun to learn. Rather than making assignments complicated, he made them useful. His lecture slides were also very useful. Great lecturer and keeps the class engaged. Definitely the best CS professor I have had at ASU and strongly recommend him. He made the class very interesting and worth your time.

The professor is fine. Honestly without his TAs this would have been a nightmare. He is kind of lazy, pulling projects/homeworks fron other teaching which can be frustrating. Lectures are normally pretty boring, with tiny writing in a huge class so if you are in the back dont bother going. He can be funny and is a good guy. Grading is fair.

The professor is chill. Both the professor and the TA were understanding and gave some leeway with assignment deadlines in this uncertain time of COVID. There was one take home midterm and the rest of the grade depended on various assignments. For the assignments, most of the things had to be searched on Google and then implemented for results.

In a sea of ASU CS professors who you can hardly understand, with cryptic schedules, assignments, and boring exams, Prof. Yang was so helpful and his lectures were engaging and easy to understand. I never felt like I was being "gotcha'd" like I have had before in other CS classes. This guy is one of the few good professors in CS at ASU.

Yang is a good professor if you can get pass him being a bit disorganized. He posts all lectures online but sometimes after class. He is a bit hard to understand but he clearly shows that he cares for the students and our learning. Good professor, I would definitely take him again.

Amazing professor, spends a good amount of time with each concept and explains them thoroughly in each lecture. Good slides and summaries too, people underestimate that! This has probably been the most difficult CSE class I've taken at ASU thus far, but I feel like I've learned the most.
Makes sure to simplify complicated concepts
shallow content, doesn't give much details,have to google eveything for project

Does a solid job of making complicated and dry topics understandable and interesting. Homework is challenging, but prepares you well for the exams which are of reasonable difficulty. Overall his grading criteria is more than fair, and if you put in the work you will definitely obtain the requisite knowledge to handle other CS classes.

I had a great experience in this class during which my team tried to solve a problem pertaining to athletics. To be honest, I never had any prior experience with python or ROS before attending this class. The class was so interesting that my team started to understand and implement the algorithms (ex: corner detection, optical flow, etc.)

Professor Blanco is one of the best professors I have taken as a CSE student at ASU. He is very passionate about natural language processing and it showed in his lectures. He would stay almost every day after class (along with office hours and Slack) and help students with any of their problems with the coding.

Professor Blanco has provided one of the best lecture experiences especially for a 400 level class. He is active by email or Slack for any inquiries about the class. For someone who knew nothing about NLP before the class, he provided a great introduction to the topic and balanced the workload fairly throughout the semester.

Easily one of the best professors at ASU. Projects/Homework is 70% of the grade; two exams worth 10% each; 5 quizzes worth 2% each and extra credit is offered on top of this incredibly generous grading scheme.  Extremely kind, loves his work. Would definitely take him again.

Best professor I've had at ASU. The course content itself is somewhat interesting, but his lecture makes it more interesting. Quizzes are relevant to exams and assignments are straightforward. It's NLP so there is more than a normal amount of reading for a CSE course, but I cannot stress how much I appreciate the effort he puts into the class.

Great intro to deep learning. No exams and only graded on homework and a project. Was very helpful and accessible in class and through Slack. Clearly wants his students to succeed. Both went into the theory and the practical usage of the topic. Used GitHub for homework submissions primarily which was amazing. One of the best CS professors at ASU.
provost magee does not respect student workers
Provost Magee hates student workers

The flipped classroom method, while not my favorite, is well executed. Despite struggling in this class, I ended up doing pretty well due to him dropping a few of your worst grades. This class is A LOT of work (a project every week) but you are given the tools needed. Don't let the first class scare you!!
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: Lots of support (TA hours everyday and extended ones on weekends). Responses to questions usually within 30 minutes. The class is extremely work heavy. You need a lot of hours outside of class. I often spend all of Friday and Saturday finishing up work (in addition to doing work throughout the week). Prof. Magee is great for CS120, though.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

review_text: Professor Magee is great! I had him for CS120, and while I found the class very difficult, the course is so well organized that the frustration came from the challenge of the work, not ambiguous instructions/grading. I felt that he clearly communicated the core concepts of the class and had an effective support structure set up to ensure success.

I enjoyed Prof Magee for Intro to CS. He is very friendly and approachable and is willing to help you when you get stuck. He also explains concepts well. This class does have a lot of daily work - there are pre-class activities 2x a week, biweekly quizzes, and weekly projects (writing computer programs). There is no textbook, which saves $
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: He is an annoying professor. He does not say about participation and attendance yet grades professionalism in the end. He says on the test to be concise in answers yet gives points based on the amount of information student provided. He does not give out much homework for the most of the semester yet throw all remaining homework for the last 2 week

I took his class during the summer. Lecture notes are clear and posted online. He is very helpful. Since it's a summer session, we have a tight schedule and homework due every week. But it's ok if you start early. He gave four quizzes and a final. You can either take four quizzes, or three highest quiz and a final. WOULD TAKEN AGAIN.

This is my favorite professor in Clark's CS department. He is very straight forward, helpful, nice, but still challenging. He does cram a lot of information into a course and goes pretty fast, but if you spend time you will learn a lot. He is very helpful when you ask, and his assignments really relate to the classwork.
I've have Magee for many classes. He is finally turning around and becoming a great professor!

Lectures basically just clarify points from the reading. Lots of super easy labs and projects that you can finish in class. Tests can be hard, but not if you do the study guide and examples in the book. He's super helpful and understanding if you have any problems. Can be boring, but you can get an A with some effort.
Very Helpful guy. He pushes you to work hard but work will be rewarded. It is very important to pay attention in class. Sometimes tests get difficult but it all averages out in the end.

Dr. Al-Khateeb is great. Class is pretty much lecture based, but if you pay attention, then it helps a lot with the tests. The weekly lab is very do-able in the hour he gives you, and he's always willing to help. He also tends to reuse questions from previous quizes and tests on his tests/final, so be sure to reference those when studying.

Solid class but it's easy to lose track of what's going on. Lecture heavy about some tough material. Weekly online labs that are easy enough. 2 test's and a final. Tests are okay. Super easy coding on it compared to the labs and 75% is history. Pay attention in the lectures and it's an easy A
Engaging during lectures, good teacher, just the right amount of work. Not a boring computer science class at all. Take him!!

Dr. Al-Khateeb was amazing! Prepares you very well for tests, and the tests themselves are straightforward-- no trick questions whatsoever. Homework and labs can be a bit tedious, but they serve a purpose! He is always down to help his students succeed. Overall, he is a great professor, and I would highly recommend him for CSC 121.

Very approachable and caring guy. He just wants you to show up (gives extra credit for attendance last couple weeks). Lectures are straightforward, weekly labs, 2 tests, and a final. Tests are 65% history and 35% coding, and the coding is very simple on tests. Class can be big and scary to say you're stuck, I just talked to him after class.

He made the material very clear through his lectures. Doing the weekly projects leaves you well-prepared for the tests. Very helpful with questions and confusion. Tests are not too hard and are a mix between application and content history. For being a required class, he made it interesting and for not previously having interest in this topic.
Great class. Nothing bad to say. He is a pretty good helper and just an overall good teacher. I will say that if you have a hard time with thick accents you may want to consider taking someone else.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: A great caring guy. The class was difficult for me because I am horrible with computers and the projects can be difficult. Get to class because the notes are important for tests and the online coding can be difficult but is hard to remember. Nevertheless, super understanding man and makes it clear that he cares about his students.
Al-Khateeb was a good professor and always helped students when needed. The class itself was kinda boring and hard to engage in but the textbook helps a lot.

For a class you have to take he makes it really interesting and as fun as he can. It is not the hardest course, show up and read the textbook and you will do fine. He is a super caring person and really appreciates people who attend, although attendance is not required. Highly recommend.

He is an iconic professor. I hate coding and struggled with it, but making use of his office hours helped explain the weekly homework assignments and quizzes. Tests are extremely difficult if you're not good at coding. I focused on vocab and short answers. Lectures are mostly based on reading PowerPoint slides and explaining the codes.

The sweetest professor, but this class is not easy. Don't just copy someone else's code. The tests are hard if you don't know the coding. There aren't many tests, but if you don't do well they will hurt your grade. I got A's on all projects, and ended w/ a B. Overall he is wonderful and the TA's are helpful, it's just hard if you don't care ab code
Dr. Al-Khateeb is very kind professor and a relatively easy grader. He expects you to do the work but is lenient. His teaching isn't the most engaging, but I overall enjoyed his class.

Dr. Al-Khateeb is a great professor and he really cares about his students. 221 is an intro class and he understands that not many people have had experience coding before. He is easy to talk to and wants to answer all your questions. Really nice.

Dr. Al-Khateeb is a genuinely good professor. Some of the reviews on here don't make a ton of sense; we did exercises everyday during class to learn the material. The textbook, while handy for practice, is not really needed as long as you participate. Participate in class, and ask questions when needed, and you'll do well.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Al-khateeb is probably the best teacher in explaining material, out of all the professors Ive came across, he was the most caring and helpful teacher of them all, his class is easy if you pay attention and just come to office hours when needing help, his office hours are so helpful. I suggest him to you guys!

Dr. Al-Khateeb was great this semester; I always enjoyed going to class. I felt that beyond explaining concepts well, he was a very caring & generous person. He was available outside of class, and he gives more than enough direction for any student to get an A. As long as you put in the effort to learn the concepts, this class will be a breeze.

Dr. Al-khateeb has been nothing but a great professor, honestly before taking the class I saw quiet bad reviews but I went ahead anyway and took it, it was so worth it, he is so caring and he tries his absolute best to make sure you understand the material. I totally recommend you guys taking his classes!

Lectures were just an exact repeat of the textbook. No coding practice just straight to the assignments after the book. They were graded harshly as well, he did not like you unless you were very good at the class. I know I'm being harsh, but take a different professor for more clear lectures and better work.

Dr. Al-Khateeb is honestly a great professor. He tries to get to know his students and genuinely cares. The projects are pretty easy and if you are struggling just ask for help over zoom and he basically does the whole thing for you. His accent takes a little time to get used to but he has good TAs too.

Dr Al-Khateeb is a good professor who is quite easy to access during his office hours. He tries his best to make sure that you truly understand the material. However, he is a very tough grader, so make sure that you follow all his instructions in labs and tests.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Tough grader

review_text: He's a good guy with a heavy accent. His class is pretty straightforward if you read the book. Lectures can be boring as he explains what you were already supposed to read. Easy A if you put in the effort.

Honestly, all of his classes are really easy. The other reviews were left by people that have no interest or background in CS. If you have any amount of experience or put in any amount of effort, you will get an A. If anything, he's too much of a pushover but he's incredibly kind. Lectures can be very slow and a lot of what feels like busy work
He's okay

I like Dr. Baker as a professor but man this is one of the hardest classes computer science majors have to take. If you work hard in this class, you will be rewarded. If you are coming after Fernandes, it will feel completely different, and the difficulty will increase a lot. I learned the most in this class and Dr. Baker will help you if you ask.

CSC 421: Tough professor, but you learn a lot. Definitely recommend taking her if you are actually interested in coding and want to get better. Uses a textbook, but you can usually find it online for free. Don't be scared to go to office hours. She records her lectures and posts them. Gives about 5 homework assignments with 2 weeks to complete it
Dr. Baker is a really tough professor, but if you work at learning stuff, she's really great.

Dr. Baker knows what she's doing very well. Articulating it to students that have never coded before is where she struggles sometimes. She is very flexible about office hours and is more than willing to help you if you seek it. The homework are very extensive but a lot of learning happens from them. The tests are not realistic either. Nice lady.

Very sweet and intelligent but not great at "dumbing it down" to an understandable level. Super flexible with office hours. Required exercises are tough, homework feels IMPOSSIBLE. Quizzes are probably one of the easier parts. Very nice person but I decided to drop a CSC kingfisher because of this class

Professor Baker means well. However, her ability to articulate concepts and reasoning is severely lacking. She is all over the place during lectures, constantly correcting herself and hesitantly answering questions. Most of the time, it feels as if she hasn't even learned the material herself.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

review_text: Dr. Baker's class is definitely very lecture dependent where I think it would be better if it was more project based. That being said, she is very helpful if you ask questions and easily accessible outside of class for anything to homework or even questions about scheduling classes, research, grad school, ect. Solid choice but you do need to try.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

review_text: She is smart and caring. She usually is pretty good on explaining and giving examples, but she is still pretty new and has so much room to grow. When you don't understand something, ASK. She will explain. Although she is young, I think she could become one of the best professors for the JMC department. DONT TURN THINGS IN LATE.

Professor Baker knows how to code, but that is about it. She isn't really good at explaining concepts and teaching in general. The way she lectures is like a less clear, straight reading of the textbook. Also, grading for homework doesn't make sense, as minuscule and large mistakes drop the score equally. Not a hard class if time is put in outside.

Professor Raicu is awesome, and very passionate even when teaching intro level courses. I now work in Data Science, and I still use tricks she taught years later. A lot of people didn't like her because the content was tough, but because the content was tough and I learned it well, it gave me a super strong foundation for other DS courses.

Condescending, talks down to students, reprimands, does not always treat students with respect. Too much lecturing, goes very fast. Accent can be hard to understand at times. Expects participation and will call on students regularly to test comprehension.
Great lectures, a lot of information in one class. Grades fairly. Final was everything we went over during class
The class topics were repetitive and not essential

Daniela is a touch teacher.  She knows what she's talking about, but it is a lot of information to take in and can be confusing.  In my opinion, the textbook is not very helpful.   The homeworks can take a very long time.  Just really make sure to go to class, take alot of notes, and it will help you with the homework.  Her grading is lenient.

Great teacher. Her classes are not easy, but she will make sure that you know the material and underlying theories. Pay attention, take an interest in the subject, and study hard and you should do well.

Dr. Raicu is one of the founders of Predictive Analytics at DePaul. She is very knowledgeable about the subject. Despite having lengthy lectures, they're interesting, beneficial & dense. If you're a PA student, I would highly recommend taking this course with/after taking CSC424 in order not to be confused. highly recommend taking course with her.
One of the worst classes I've had in the MSPA program. But it's required. Get ready to be confused a lot.

Daniela is a really good professor and passionate about her work. She emphasizes concepts and understanding of the material so make sure you understand why you are doing something instead of just regurgitating examples (which you shouldn't anyway). Learned so much this quarter.

Professor Raicu is passionate about this subject, unfortunately, that passion does not mean she can teach effectively. The prepared materials don't reflect the material that seems to be important to be successful in the exam.  On top of that, the only exam, the final, is 40 % of the final grade. Be very prepared. It will make or break your grade.

Wasn't what I expected - this class is mainly statistics and learning the mathematical process of data mining. She was extremely passionate about the content, had lots of examples, very willing to work with students/extend hmwk, and did lab tutorials. Midterm and final are mainly theory based! Make sure you understand concepts, partial credit given

Prof. Raicu is extremely helpful, passionate and knowlegable about her field. Fair grader, gives extra credit a lot. Doesn't take attendance. Your whole grade is based on your midterm, final and homework. No textbook. She gives students one-on-one attention during class and is very helpful. Boring lectures.

She's very intelligent and knows her field well, but is not clear in teaching less leartn individuals. For her Data Analysis II class, she used powerpoints that were a bit confusing; I found nearly all my help in the textbook even though the book itself was one of the hardest I ever used. She's very friendly and willing to help though.

She is definitely knowledgeable, but that doesn't mean she is a good teacher. It's unfortunate, because her power points are hard to follow without her direction. Secondly, the text book is probably more useful than her direction, however she doesn't use it all. Basically, I ended up teaching myself the class.

Passionate and very good at her field, encouraging students to do research with her.  Excellent slides, fair homework assignments and exams.  One of the best teachers at DePaul.  You will learn a lot in her classes.

She makes you calculate formulas by hand(without using functions on caculator) homework takes days this way. If you understand what the formula does, I do not see why you have to do it by hand. For an INTRO class Excel needs to be used more than the Advanced SAS program.
NOT helpful at all. The tests cover material the text doesn't. Neither are her powerpoints any good. The class was a waste of time.

Handwriting on whiteboard is hard to read. Needs to improve on explaining requirements to complete SAS assignments. Lecture is VERY organized. Makes time to help students. The book for the course needs to be replaced or revised.

Easy if you do the work and get lucky with a decent group for the final project. Professor repeatedly made it clear that he is very busy and the class suffers for it. Poor communication, very few assignments, and grades took forever. The prerecorded lectures and 4 hws are good, but the group project is poorly executed. Overall would not recommend.

Horrendous in terms of help and being available to his students. Never responds to emails. Lectures are common sense, redundant, and very theoretical. All coding and syntax will be learned on your own time, he teaches the most basic functions yet the homework is 20x harder. I do not understand the need for this professor nor class.

He gives unlimited attempts to pass quizzes and posts answers so it's an easy 100%. For midterm, he allowed a double sided page of notes. Midterm curved. Instead of a final, we did a group project. Overall, very fair professor. Awesome tutorials. But tough homework grader. Clear structure. You will pass if you do the work!

Dr. Brown's class is tough but fair. He has detailed modules with high quality lectures, weekly tutorials and M/C quizzes. He's always happy to help via email. There are 5 HW assignments and a final. If the subject matter is new, watch AND take notes on the modules. Start the HW ASAP, passing the class depends on its completion.

This is a fundamentals course but taught as if we already knew the information. When you reach out with questions, he tells you to talk to other students instead of helping. I spent an average of 30-40 hours on homework assignments. Half of the class failed the final, and he sent an email telling us it was not his fault people failed.

Dr. Brown was awesome. DSC 465 was fully async - and he had detailed modules set up with high quality lectures. He's always happy to help via email. There's a quiz every week, a midterm, 4 large HW assignments and a group project at the end. If you watch all the lectures, you'll be fine. Definitely start the HWs at least a week in advance.
rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: To be totally honest, I think he's a fine lecturer. The workload is insane and disrespectful to anyone with a job (most graduate students). He warns in the syllabus that this is a lot - but I don't know any grad students who have 20 extra hours for one class. He even says don't take another class with this. Most of us don't have a say in that.

Not a very good professor. Disorganized lecturer, slow responses for online students, and an inconsistent grader (never knew what I got wrong on exams which make up most of the grade). He definitely favored in class students. Would not recommend. Go with someone who knows their stuff.

Dr. Brown is a great choice for 465 due to his expertise in the field. Concepts are fairly straight forward and grading is fair. There are four homework assignments, a midterm, and group final project - which all make for a very manageable workload for a graduate level course. He's very accessible and always willing to help.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Group projects

review_text: Professor knows the topic very well but the "labs" were not really labs and we were told a few times to just read online how to graph on our own time. Slides only included graphs and barely any text so studying for a midterm was very impractical. Prepare to spend a lot of time working on the homework assignments.

He is disorganized at best. The homework never accurately reflected what was gone over in class. I wish we had gone through more material because the first 5 weeks was insanely slow. I was happy that R and SPSS were used concurrently but he could have taken more time to learn R himself.

when he showed us how to make a dendrogram that superimposes boxes that show how many clusters were made in R...i was floored. in a good way. He's either a really good R programmer (which I suspect) or he's done heavy preparation in making the coding portions of the class really good and useful.

I'm taking this course in tandem with CSC 424 (in R) and I really like how it fill in some of the holes from the other course. This quarter, Eli is also showing us how to do things in R, which is REALLY NICE! So right now, I like learning R from two angles. The homework is manageable and the lectures are good.

I took IS 467 with this professor. From my understanding it was his first time teaching this class, he did OK. There was too much repetition on some topics and too much time spent 'advertising' other classes. Approachable if you have questions, but a bit confusing in explanations. Lectures were behind and we did not get through all material.

It's Sartipi, and if you are considering this elective then you've taken OS with him, so you know by now that he can't teach. However, this class is way easier than OS because the quizzes are considerably easier and he grades the projects you do super leniently. The most annoying things are the required attendance and group meetings.

He is not very good at teaching the material, at times it feels like he doesn't even know what he's talking about. In the first exam is bad, almost non of the material he covers in class is on it. Instead you need to match what he has on the slides to the textbook and read most of the book to understand it.

If you want to pass, just ignore everything he talks about in class and learn the topics yourself from the textbook. His lectures are just him reading word for word from useless and confusing powerpoints. The quizzes are incredibly difficult as well, and the class averages were around 50 percent. If you have to take him, im so sorry.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

review_text: He makes it obvious that he is a prof for the research to the exclusion of even trying to be a good teacher. Since I have an A, I will say that to do well you need to ignore EVERYTHING he says and just read the textbook/watch Neso Academy's OS playlist. Only upside is that he is so dogwater (he knows, but doesn't care) that he curves grades hard.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

review_text: "he has no rules, no boundaries. He doesn't flinch at torture, human trafficking or genocide. He's not loyal to a country or a flag or any set of ideals. He trades blood for money. He's your new best friend."   -Basically Sartipi
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: glorified slide reader

Surprisingly not a bad class with him. I took OS with him and it was miserable but this class really wasn't as bad and I learned a lot of good stuff. The quizzes are still difficult but not to the extent of OS. If you've taken a class with him before and worried about taking this class, you should sign up. This class is much better than OS.
rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: He is an awful professor, who doesn't respond to emails, all he does is read off a PowerPoint all day, and the quizzes cover topics not covered in class. One dude had his dad die, it took an email TO THE CHAIR OF THE COMPSCI DEPT for this professor to finally respond. This professor seems to be more concentrated in his research than he is to teach
HORRIBLE PROFESSOR AVOID AT ALL COSTS. WORST EXPERIENCE EVER
You get quizzes includes a whole chapter of 70 pages you have to memorize with no cheat sheet. You And Your Mind ALONE

Absolute dog water teacher. He will "teach" next to nothing and test for an incredible level of understanding. Essentially impossible to prepare for tests/quizzes. Avoid at all costs. Multiple semesters of students have complained to the department chair. They "talk to him" and nothing changes. Abysmal professor.

Things you should know before taking ANY course with Dr.Sartipi:  He will give little to no feedback. He is not accessible outside of class. (Will read your email and not respond). Will give unengaging lectures. Will NOT learn how to make software.  Since being tenured, he does not care about teaching anymore. Avoid this professor like the plague.

I believe that Dr. Sartipi is smart, but it does not translate to the classroom. Often he gets confused what he's saying or will explain things wrong. Quizzes are difficult and study material given will not prepare you. I would recommend avoiding if you can.

This class is one of the worst classes I have ever taken. He teaches in the worst way possible and the study guides are useless for the quizzes and tests. Do not take a class with him! You are better off watching YouTube

The study guides that are given before tests are useless. You are better off learning the material on your own instead of relying on the lectures. He is incredibly difficult to follow along with and if you make one or two bad grades, it's almost impossible to recover.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: The absolute worse. This man cannot teach. He puts no effort into students. You will have to self teach everything because this man is a poor excuse of a teacher. Doesn't even deserve to call himself a professor, it's disrespectful to other professors. He is usually the only option for this class so best of luck to any poor soul who takes him.

He is the epitome of lazy and ineffective teaching practices. Reads directly off of the PowerPoint, assigns 80-100 practice problems a section(not chapter), and just tells you to do them, literally puts 0 effort into this course, and reiterates that "it's not hard" to make you feel bad about yourself. Take literally anyone else, please.

Worst professor in this department. Horrible "lectures' that just go on a tangent and never end. Powerpoint slides for anything that is a lecture. Not helpful at all, and tends to fight with students about who is right.

He was not a good professor AT ALL. He read off the powerpoints and never taught us anything, yet expected us to understand the material. He was also a really tough grader and never communicated with the TA's.

Dr. Sartipi's lecture is definitely not easy because the material itself is super confusing. He puts a lot of emphasis in solving problems in the textbook and usually put the even questions in his exams. I find his teaching style easy to follow since I study using the textbook a lot. If you're like me, I say take his course! He's caring when 1:1.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Caring

review_text: He just reads from powerpoints he didn't make then tests you on specific information based on the vague topics he told you to go study. Teaches like you should have a background in operating systems despite it not being a prerequisite. For reference, my entire class' average on the final after I took it was in the 50%'s...
do not recommend

Boring, tedious and slide-based lectures. He did not write the slides and it's clear he didn't read them either. Constantly changes requirements and expectations for group projects and tests. Is either unable or unwilling to help students with problems with HIS provided code. He's consistent at being inconsistent. I do not recommend him.

If you show up, and put minimal effort into studying the powerpoints / book / assignments, you'll get an easy A. People do poorly because they treat the class like a joke (which it is). However, don't expect to learn anything in his course, he is very vague on every single thing he teaches, and provides similar vague feedback on any project.

This guy is a worst case scenario professor. This guy's lectures consist of reading slides he obviously didn't write, flashing prewritten programs on the board for 5 seconds and then blaming us for not understanding the material. At least that's what I think is happening because this man obviously learned english from the back of a cereal box.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: Here's the bad. He simply cannot teach the material he is meant to teach the class and tests are extremely difficult and the questions are hard to read; however, this was his first time teaching the course. For the good: Outside of class he's nice. Gives decent feedback for projects and papers. Gave a little extra credit for attending seminars.
Worst professor I have ever had. Doesn't understand the material himself. Would not recommend.

CSCI 4000 is a waste of time. Its a 1 credit hour course required to graduate. He makes you do ridiculous group presentations and group papers. He reads from a PowerPoint when he actually does lecture (majority of class is taken up by group presentations). Tests are mostly free response but are taken from his PowerPoint.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

review_text: Sartipi is honestly the worst professor ECU's computer science department has. He will give you a midterm study guide with points just referencing chapters. The night before at 11pm he will finally email you telling you what he claims will be on the test. The test was nothing like he said it was and he even got his own information wrong. Awful.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: Dr Gudivada is a great profess in charge of teaching a rather difficult and mathematically loaded subject. Watch the lectures, take notes, do the practice problems and homework you'll be fine. He'll work with you if you're having trouble understanding. I took a point off because I do think the math could be taught in a more abstract manner.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

review_text: Easy Tests and Assignments. Need to put an extra effort for programming assignments. Easy to Score A grade.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

review_text: amazing lectures, can score well if you understand the basics and follow the assignments.

I have taken Information retrieval class, his programming assignments needs a lot of work but worth the time. His written assignments are quite straight forward. overall I am satisfied with the course
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Inspirational

review_text: he teaches very well and the assignments are very good. very flexible, attendance not required, can score well if you listen to his lectures

Took Information Retrieval course this semester which was pretty interesting and lot of new things to learn. Teaches Really good and even his assignments are easy to score a good grade. If you are looking for A grade, then make sure you take his courses whenever offered.

I took Machine Learning last semester and that was a fun class. I learnt alot from his class and also from his assignments. Gives sufficient time for assignments and postpones the deadlines if needed. Teaches really good and gives good grades.
explains well and gives good grades
he teaches well, his home works are useful to get a clear idea on the topics present in the course. attendance is not mandatory. he is caring and flexible for students.
rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: I love Dr. Herndon. He is one of the best professors you will ever have. He is easily available outside of class, he is constantly looking for feedback during his classes, and trying to improve his teaching. He is one of the most inspiring professors I've ever had. His classes are difficult but we are CS majors, so it makes sense.
This class has a final project instead of the final exam and a lot of practice activities, but you need to pay attention and do everything on time.
Lots of hands on homework and a final project, no midterm or final. Very fun class. 10/10 would take Dr. Herndon again.
Took this professor twice, really enjoyed his classes and felt level of difficulty was perfect.

For all the effort he puts into teaching, this should be an online class. In person he explains nothing, just has in class exercises that last a few minutes and expects you to know the language he just told you about but didn't teach. In this class you learn about multiple languages but he gives zero instruction on how to run them yourself.

Not good at explaining new concepts, explains in very confusing ways. Briefly skims over any subject, then is confused why you don't know much about it. Tests are weighted so heavily that other assignments barely matter, and if you fail either the midterm or the final, it's over.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Tough grader

review_text: Professor Herndon is very dedicated to his students & research! He gives straight forward lectures and always clear explanations with the opportunity for questions. If you study and participate you will do well.

Herndon will hit you with assignments from other professor without giving the assignments they had leading up to it. He also went over a programming language very briefly and then made over half the final exam be questions about that language specifically. He expects you to understand without properly teaching the material

One of the best professors I have ever had! I highly recommend taking his classes when they are an option.  I had him for computer org and I don't think many people could make such a boring topic as interesting as he did.  He feels bad because he thinks he is boring you, but if you really pay attention I promise you will love his classes!!

A lot of students like Tarnoff. He is very relate-able and you can tell he works hard to present himself that way. Teaching is great, tests are fine. Not too streesful but not a breeze either. His class inspired me to pursue some electric circuit building. Would recommend.
Took him for Computer Organization. Very knowledgeable, very funny and makes learning the material a breeze. He wrote the textbook for the class but gives you the PDF for free. Would take again.
Tarnoff is the man. His server side course is not easy but you will learn the material if you want to make an A. He gives little extra credit, but if you work hard you will do well.

Tarnoff is a great professor and person.  I took him for multiple classes throughout my stay at ETSU.  His iOS class set me on a professional career path.  He was also a fantastic advisor.  I would recommend taking as many of his classes as you can.  He is not a classically trained programmer and honestly, it's a nice change.

Probably the best teacher in the department. Very helpful, tries to bring a bit of humor to otherwise dry material. Willing to take time out of class to explain complex concepts 5 different ways if necessary. If you get the chance to take a Tarnoff class, do it.
Great professor! He is very helpful and has an interesting personality, which helps keep me interested even when the lectures are not interesting.

Take him for every class that you can, he is the best in the department. Explains things 10 different ways so everyone understands, study guides are last semesters actual test (and yours are very similar.
Hands down best teacher on campus. Excellent sense of humor, does a great job covering some complex, boring material. Stops class to address questions. If you have the chance to take him do it!

Great teacher! Usually keeps things pretty interesting in class. Doesn't take attendance, but I would recommend going anyways. There are very few assignments throughout the semester.. tests are easy if you look over the old sample tests he posts!
Likes to have fun in class. Learn a lot without too much work. Best part is he post's old tests online with answers for you to study. Highly recommended!

I enjoy his class. Tests are easy if you study the provided study guides (ie. previous years tests). Quizzes are sometimes tricky but are rather straight-forward. He makes jokes about computers to keep the material interesting. Great guy!

He is an absolutely stellar teacher.  Very clear about what he expects and doesn't mind taking time out of class to help with work.  Reasonable and keeps lectures at least somewhat interesting with stories.  Took him for 1250 and 2150 and he is my favorite CSCI professor.
David Tarnoff is a great teacher.  Some of the material covered in Comp. Org. is a bit dry, but he makes it interesting.  I really recommend taking this professor for any class he is teaching.

Fantastic professor.  Has a lot of experience in the hardware field, and it shows.  Really knowledgeable and yet relates extremely well to students.  Interesting even when teaching extremely dry material.  Self-admitted obsession with NAND gates not a detriment.  Wish he taught more courses so I could take him every semester.

David "NAND gate" Tarnoff is awesome teacher. Although I found the subject interesting to begin with, he makes the subject as interesting as possible to the rest of the class. He integrates real world applications into his teaching. Looking over the old tests was helpful, so make sure and do that. Quite humorous too.
Mr Tarnoff is great his classes are useally very enjoyable. Never thought binary would be so easy.

Great teacher! Probably the best in the department. He's easy to understand, interesting, and always willing to help students. His website is a great reference even after you've finished his class. He provides you with everything you need to succeed.
His teaching methods are very straight-forward and comes right out of the book that he published for the class. His humor is always nice to have when its early in the morning.

Attendance is recommended because of the subject, but not a chore since he is committed to making the class as interesting as possible.  Pay attention, ask questions, (he is always available)and you should do well in any of his classes
Great teacher!  He is always there to help.

This guy is amazing. He understands lecture is boring and he does everything to make it enjoyable. Plus his illustrations are spot on and his teaching style is top notch.  He cares more about your comprehension of the subject than your test scores. So less tests and more learning. Seriously... take this guy for everything you can.

Tarnoff is GREAT!  Some of the material covered in his 2150 class can be confusing, almost arcane. However, he explains it all very well.  He is helpful and understanding, as well as funny and entertaining!
I have this teacher right now and I think that he is the best teacher I have so far. Very funny and explains everything to the last detail. I hope that I can have him for my other CSCI classes.
Take this professor, you won't regret it.

Tarnoff is one of the best teachers in the CS dept. He explains everything very thouroghly and makes sure every students gets the concepts before moving on. I wish he taught more classes so I could avoid the other ****'s that should bow to David's feet!

Both classes cover hard material, however, he does a great job of explaining everything. He reviews the material often and ALWAYS stops for questions. He also has a good sense of humor which is hard to come by in a computer science teacher. Great guy I would def. recommend bc he makes it as interesting as possible.

David Tarnoff is the man!  Clearly explains concepts and goes through numerous examples.  Does, in fact, include humor in lectures.  If he didn't they could get boring.  I gave easiness a 4.  His exams for comp org are not easy easy, but not difficult either.  Just understand the concepts.
LUV HIM!

Funny guy. His lectures are pretty decent, he uses alot of jokes and humor while explaining things. Always has something funny to relate his lecture to. His tests are a bit difficult, but he is more than willing to help with anything you have trouble with.

He'll explain things a hundred different ways to make sure everyone gets it.  His lectures aren't boring b/c he uses a lot of humor while he's explaining things.  Lab assignments aren't too bad, but his tests are a little more difficult than they should be.
Teaches EVERYTHING on a very low level.  Understand NAND gates and FLIP-FLOPS, or you will be in trouble in ALL of his classes (even programming classes).

Tarnoff has about 10 different ways/analogies he uses to explain anything, so if you don't get it the first go 'round, he'll find a way to help you understand. Great personality, fun to joke around with. Makes a rather bland subject interesting.
Hes great!I think he is the most organized and the most smartest of all teachers in the computer science department. He know it all!!!, just be astowned at the amount of material on his website.
I thoroughly enjoyed being in his class. Very nice person, knows his stuff and always willing to help. Good sense of humor.
knows his stuff inside and out.  very willing to help out
No Comments
friendly, willing to help students out, makes and hands out great notes, no surprises on tests. Makes hard to understand concepts relatively easy to learn.

A challenging class that requires critical thinking and time devotion, but will make you a better programmer at the end. Do the labs to completion every week & save them for reference. Use lecture/lab slides to get almost everything needed to do new labs/projects. Study for tests based on given outlines. Start on projects as soon as assigned.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

review_text: He's tough but you learn a lot. Had him for CSCI3110.

Had this teacher for multiple classes. Verdict: AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!! 100% HONESTY, he is one of the hardest teachers to please. Grading is WAY TOO CRITICAL, no feedback for assignments on what was wrong or why, has THE WORST late assignment policy(30%day), & lectures were USELESS for his UNNECESSARILY HARD assignments. Would not recommend EVER!!

With little knowledge of PHP coming in, I was at a loss for the first half of the semester. Learning hard code construct is to be done on your own time, and you'll be looked at sort of like an idiot if you ask for help often enough. Labs weren't graded, but many tests and projects throughout. Really, he's okay, minus the syntax issue.

I went to his office several times.  He never once helped me and instead answered my direct questions with a question.  He WILL not help you with homework or labs at all.  Be prepared for a terrible class.
He's okay.  I think he's interested in me learning the subject.

he is nice as a person.but he shouldnt teach.i dont recommend this proffesor if you really want to learn something but if you care only about grade then you have to put on your test paper or in your project absolutely whatever he told in the class.your idea and way wont give you good grade.he is so far from real life software developing(i took 1250

No focused on lecture,he lost point of lecture and spread the lecture ,doesnt follow a way to teach.he mixes the lectures.sometimes he wastes the time and talking about previous easy lectures appereantly he comes unprepared.lectures and quizes not too bad but he always find a reason to not to give you the point.when you ask he even cant explain why

I really like Prof. Roach.  He is from Jamaica, and has a noticeable accent, but not something that is hard to overcome.  I found the class very easy, but part of it is probably my past programming experience.  Tests and quizzes aren't incredibly difficult.  I would recommend him.

Nice guy, but tends to go through the material too quickly.  The projects are extremely detailed requiring information we have not yet covered.  Lenient grading scale is a plus though.  I would really only recommend this prof. if you have prior programming knowledge.

Had no trouble understanding his accent. But his programming projects were ridiculous. Took way too much time; spare time that a full-time student shouldn't have anyway. Projects often needed stuff that we hadn't even covered in class. Often singled students out and displayed their mistakes. Lenient grading, though.

He's from Jamaica so there is a small language barrier at the beginning. Quickly overcome that. He jumps around on hard topics and reiterates the easy topics. Avoid this prof if you actually want to learn the material. Take him if you want an easy grade.
Took Mr. Roach for the game programming class. He's a nice guy, knows the material, and is willing to help (and NOT hard to understand).
Once you get used to his accent, you don't even notice it.
He clearly knows and loves the subject, and does a very good job of conveying his knowledge to others. Thorough and informative, even if his tests are a bit inordinately long.
Very helpful professor, takes the time to make sure students understand the material

Notes are clear and to the point.  Very knoweldgeable and conveys points effectively even with a strong accent :p  Only con is that the programming tests were all written and no lab, but that is probably a departmental issue.

Very effective teaching method.  Tests are extremely vague and unclear.  Midterm consisted of creating unspecified Rose diagrams and a req analysis. Final had 55 points on two questions...  Didn't know them, got screwed.

I heard he was a pretty good undergraduate professor. Mr. Roach is a HORRIBLE graduate professor. Has no knowledge of the subject and is very disorganized. A complete waste of time and money.  Very disappointed
A little hard to understand, but a nice person and willing to help
OK, If you can understand his slur of english it might be an easier class.
Explains concepts. Prepared for class.
Very helpful. Classes are very well prepared. Engages students.
Really loved this class!
Great Course.  Has high expectations, but I got a great job based on the things I learned in this class!

I was dreading having to take this class, but it was actually pretty easy!  Not a bad teacher at all; lectures are usually pretty boring but the tests come primarily from the powerpoints. Use good grammar and spelling and the assignments won't be a problem!
Great Teacher, this is not just an easy A however he is great. Better than most teachers.

Very interesting class, though it was also very intense. 3.5 week schedule with four 5 page papers, one 15 page research paper. Dr. Pittarese's standards are very high for papers so be prepared to spend time editing and reediting your papers. Dr. Pittarese is tough, but fair. Overall good teacher.
Very informative class. Be prepared to exhibit a professional attitude in this class.

I really enjoyed his educational style, and he is very clear about what he expects and what you need to do.  Keep track of his labs and study for the quizzes, as they are the harder parts of the class as compared to the tests.  Make sure to do your best on the project as well.  Highly recommended as a CSCI elective.
My favorite class so far at ETSU. Fun simulation. Practical for future experience. Helped me a lot in job interviews. But yeah, I agree with the last reviewer, if you're not in class you do miss out.

CSCI 3720 was an absolute waste of time, he teaches you nothing, it's a data entry class. If you miss one day of class and he has a quiz on the day you miss 10% of your grade, if you walk in after he hands out quizzes again you miss the quiz. Oh and he starts class earlier than it's supposed to. This class was a waste of my time

I would recommend Dr. Pittarese for CSCI 2910. Straight-forward in his class. If you need help he is always available. Great communication skills. I had no prior knowledge of PHP. Lectures are recorded in case you have to miss class. I agree with the two previous comments as well. A+ Teacher!!

Taking Server Side Programming with Pittarese was fairly straight forward. The previous comment holds true. Fast moving, but if you understand logic that you should have learned in 1250, you will be alright.
Simple class. No textbook used. Instead you use php.net documentation. Moves pretty fast but easy to understand if you have your CS logic down. Quiz at beginning of every lab.

Informative, fun, and challenging. Lectures give all information needed to do well on tests. Labs are very extensive (and may often turn into homework) but are great practice exercises and good reference for projects. Prof provides clear expectations for what should be done on tests/projects. Class requires decent understanding of SQL & ERD.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

review_text: I made an account for this, so that means I care. Bennett was a god send for my grade. He is beyond helpful outside of class. Would smash 10/10

Bennett is a great dude and a great teacher. He tries to avoid powerpoints where possible, but the lectures where he does are incredibly boring and dull. He'll help you outside of class if you approach him. He has a great wit and is very understanding.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

review_text: Brilliant, yet bestows his knowledge in an easy to digest manner.

Bennett knows his topics are boring and hard, and tries to not make them that way. He's definitely helpful when you have issues. but you have to ask him. He won't come to you. When he grades things he's very descriptive and points out issues/suggestions. I definitely love having him as a professor. Yes, the classes are boring, but he's a great guy.

He tries to make discrete math fun, but it just cannot be.  He is super nice and his class would be fine if it weren't math.  Some of the sections are probably hard to teach.  He will go over things as many times as you ask, but you probably still won't understand.  He is always willing to give extra help if you request it.

Dr. Choi is the best, and he truly cares about his students and provides a solid foundation in research methodology. The workload is a bit heavier than typical 300/400 level courses, but it is manageable if you show up to class and talk often with him/TA. If you put in the effort, you'll walk away with a high grade and a great project.
Dr. Choi is great if you want a rigorous but not punishing experience in research practicum. He is super personable and helpful outside of class. I can see why he is a popular professor.

Excellent professor. The best class I've taken at Emory. +AI practicum is incredible, highly reccomend.  You'll do a lot of work, but if you can do that, it's incredibly incredibly rewarding. This man pushes you in a way no other professor has done so far for me at this school. Joined his lab afterwards.   Take the class.

Dr. Choi is probably the most popular CS professor at Emory and most feedback on him are mixed, but in the end folks I know don't regret taking his course. Working hard is just his creed in life so he also pushed it upon his students, but if you reach out to him he can always help you out/give extensions.

No tests, only coding quizes and assignments. Great but difficult professor and requires a lot of work outside classes - pays off for later CS classes such as CS326, other CS253 sections w/ easier classes suffer later on

His lectures are good but he just reads off the textbook(a website he wrote). Graded by 10 quizzes and 3 hws - quizzes are not difficult but hws literally take a week to complete. Every time ppl complain abt workload he'll dismiss them by saying "welcome to my life :)" This course makes you wonder if you really wanna be a CS major.
Looks like he's been through a lot at UPenn
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: He is a nice person overall, but there is no need to make a 2-level course so hard, and his TAs managed to make it even harder by their grading criteria

Decent lecturer, cares a lot, BUT the amount of work this class takes is INSANE  He asked in class if this takes up more time then every other class combined, and 3/4ths of the class raised their hands. Prepare to spend 10-15 hours a week on this class if you take it. Also, IntelleJ and the gitbook takes a hugeee learning curve. Very hard at first

Expect this class to be hard and expect to spend a lot of time on it because it is designed to be that way. Being responsible about when you start assignments and getting them done is imperative to your success in this course. Falling behind will make the class exponentially harder. I found Dr. Choi and his course to be incredibly rewarding.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lots of homework

review_text: Expects us to have a way deeper understanding than we actually would coming out of CS 171. To pass you either have to teach yourself, or already know the content for the class beforehand. Examples and textbook make no sense to a beginner.

It is understandable that he makes this course so hard because he clearly wants to increase our level, but with all his useless reading of his show-off code the entire time, this course brings only physiological aversion.

Professor Choi's lectures consist of him reading from his own GitHub book, which lacks information and clarity. The quizzes and homework are insanely difficult. His code is hard to read especially for someone who just took CS171. Overall, I think this class makes students question whether they want to do CS or not.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

review_text: His "textbook" is essentially pages of code and very short comments. The code is written to industry standards making it confusing to understand. It's not rare to spend hours understanding his code, syntax, or whatever random System methods he used before beginning an assignment. Office hours are PACKED, extra credit near end of sem though.
Prof. Choi is very passionate about his field. Love his lectures. He is also super accessible in office hours and cares about this students. Definitely my favorite professor last semester!
CS 385 - AI Research Practicum is probably the best course in the CS department! Dr. Choi is an amazing professor!

Dr. Choi proves himself that he is the hero we need. The class material is super easy and comprehendible even with a negative IQ. As a Python beginner, quizzes are generally doable and don't really require much knowledge from DSA. The group project plays a dominant part in the course, so be prepared to cram and grab some tissues if you need to cry.
Professor Choi is a very nice person outside of classroom. He really cared about students' learning. However, his assignments could be difficult and take a long time to do but not impossible.

Dr. Choi is certainly a tough grader. But apart from the fact that you might receive low grades, his class is perfect. Projects are difficult but they will get you well prepared for the coding interview. He is also very helping and cares about his students' learning. You will end up learning a lot. Not the hero we deserve but the hero we need.
He's a good teacher but the class is really hard, he teaches off a git book and he just read it in class word for word.

Not clear about group semester project and Doesn't matter if u do 100% on all the HWs, project is most important thing. HWs are very challenging, and he is available but his office hours are so full it can be difficult to get enough time with him for help. Def ask lots of questions bc his coding is pretty complicated. He's good only if u ask help.

He teaches in a way that is only understandable if you already have a strong foundation in data structures, so if CS 171 is your only prior experience with data structures this class can be challenging, but NOT impossible. Just be prepared to learn concepts by yourself by watching youtube videos, reading articles and asking questions!

His class was taught in an industry-preparation way rather than an actual theory-based way. The way he/his TAs answer the students' questions was very ambiguous and did not really help. It was really challenging to understand the algorithms with his textbook, and therefore additional web search was needed, which took extra amount of time.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: Dr. Choi's class is the hardest CS class I've taken. He is really smart, and he does care a lot about how we do. That being said, I think for most people, this class is too much. The "textbook" that he uses is one that he created and to do the homework/quiz most of us find it insufficient and have to consult the web. Only take if u have extra time.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: He's rlly smart -> can be condescending; students are scared to ask questions/help. I go to office hrs a lot, found out he's a genuine guy who cares about the students learning. The TAs respond to community questions more (not good answers tbh). Quizs and hws pretty difficult/overwhelming. Recorded lectures are good. Craves student participation.

He works so hard to prepare lectures that goes over Computer Science concepts. The main complaint is homework assignments/ tests are too difficult. He's truly inspirational and get students to code. He's the best professor I've had at Emory. He gives CS students wise advice in his office hours on how to approach their career. Definitely take him!
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

review_text: Best.

One of the better professors in the dept. Problems are relevant in the CS discipline and in CS job interview questions. Wish he taught more courses and made it a bit more difficult. Homeworks are fun and lectures are concise and helpful. Take this course!
Not the hero we deserve but the hero we need
Best thing to happen to Emory CS.
Very knowledgeable in NLP. Wished that this class was more focused on machine learning. Definitely a unique elective course worth taking.

He is one of the greatest CS professor at Emory. He is brilliant and really cares of his student. He makes sure you get the grade you deserves. He is always there if you need help with course material.

Good lecturer and really cares about what you learn more than what you already know. One of the most important class to take not just for computer science majors. People complain that his class is hard, but they obviously don't put in the work and just complain.
Hard grader and hard tests. Cares about students success and really will take time to learn everyones name and if you go to his office hours he will sit with you until you understand the material

I loved this class. For a CS elective, it was super light on the workload, and it was genuinely interesting! There were weekly research paper discussions, three manageable HWs early on in the semester, and a research project with a presentation. He offers a lot of good feedback. Enthusiastic professor and interesting guest lectures!
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

review_text: This class was just ok. Good intro to the research Dr. Aviv does, but it's a lot of work: weekly readings and responses, 3 HWs, and a significant project (no exams). Grading is easy, but have to do the work and requirements are sometimes unclear. Aviv is an ok professor and good lecturer, but can be a little rude and condescending at times.

Honestly was surprised by the negative reviews here. Aviv is a very str8 forward professor. Exams are hard but if you study hard you'll do well and there's always chances for extra cred. Office hrs saved my life. Hardest part was switching to java - so if you forgot how to code in java just study up before this class and you'll b fine.

While Prof. Aviv's late policy is good, don't rely on it, and start on labs when you can. However, there's a lot of extra credit available on labs/projects so do it when you can. Tests are curved and only account for 20% of the grade. The lowest lab is dropped. Go to office hours right before it starts and don't be afraid to ask questions.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: i hate this guy

Professor Aviv! Is not a good professor. He believes all his students should be professional on the subject he is teaching. He is a very strict grader. Exams are literally impossible and the course is heavy loaded with assignments (worksheets, labs and projects). He does not help his students, and is not understandable with any situation.
He knows a lot about the subject he is teaching, however he is the least understanding professor I have had in my life. He is so strict and not understandable at all.
