The other half of class is used for lab, which doesn't prepare you for the 3 exams that account for 30% of your final grade. It's very hard.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

review_text: Computer science isn't an easy subject, but Dr. Barbosa is careful to make sure his students understand the material. The lectures are essential to the class and your learning, so if you feel lost or confused, use him as a resource. I've had friends who have taken other professors and struggled, so I highly recommend this one.

I couldn't pay attention because of how condescending he lectures the class. The OLA assignments aren't helpful for learning code, even starting with Python is a bad curriculum. Even so, Dr. Barbosa was redundant in each session and didn't cover new information in good depth.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

review_text: Is this class hard? Yeah kinda. Does Dr. Barbosa explain things well? Absolutely and even if there is a topic that still isnt clear, ask and he will do everything in his power to help you understand. He makes it known on the first day and reminds you continually that he wants you to succeed and genuinely means it.

Barbosa is an excellent lecturer, encourages you to ask questions, and really wants his students to understand the material. You still have to work for it, but his lectures directly correlate with the book (he uses the textbook power points). One of my best decisions was to take him for my first ever Computer Science class! Would take again!
He's the best computer science professor that I have ever taken. Very approachable and always there to help. highly recommend him.

He's a great CS1 teacher. He really tries in algorithms, but most of what I learned about algorithms came from the book, youtube, or Dr. Pettey in Discrete Structures. The first half of the semester was pretty much CS3.

I didn't get textbook and was fine because everything was on online slides and it is a great class for notetaking. Lowest test score was dropped which was really good. Pop quizzes stopped being hard when I read last weeks notes start of every class. I really liked his code examples during class, and there was always good feedback on anything wrong.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Barbosa is a great teacher, and I would highly recommend taking his class if available.  Makes it easy to take notes, which really help out with the homework. Follows the book as well so if you miss you can read the chapter to get an idea of what to do.  The tests can be challenging,  just make sure to read the questions thoroughly.

I found that reading the book helped a lot in this course. He follows the textbook, so that is super helpful. He's great at explaining things, and he won't make you feel dumb for asking questions. He is pretty funny, even at 8 in the morning. The multiple choice questions on his tests can be pretty difficult, but the textbook reviews help a lot.
Sometimes his quiz and test questions threw me off, but overall he's a decent teacher and his homework is fairly easy.
Tries to cram two lectures worth of material into one class. Has not taught for very long.

Dr. Al-Tobasei cares for his students. Java exams were programming questions on paper which makes it difficult if you're on a tight schedule or can't allocate enough time for CSCI3033. Attending class gives you a participation grade. During the Fall 2025 exams, he allowed a 1 page (1 side) handwritten, cheat sheet. Getting the textbook helps a ton!

Tobasei is a good guy. He understands your issues and is reasonable. He grades fairly and this course is very easy. Lecture is okay, not very engaging but is clear and explained well. He wants you to succeed and is a good intro to the computer science department.

His assignments and requirements are unclear at times. Reads off of powerpoint slides the whole time and goes over pre-made code. Exams feel like it was made the night before. I thought I would've enjoyed learning a new language, but his teaching style made it difficult.  If you have the option, take someone else.

Avoid...lol. Lectures are very dry because he is reading straight off of the power points. With that in mind, go ahead and pull up the youtube channel, "Programming with Mosh" and I guarantee you will learn way more there than attending class. Unless if you're in a bulking season from all the stress eating you're going to do, take another class.

Probably one of the worst professor I have taken. His lectures are quite dry and his style of teaching is ineffective. He basically reads off the powerpoint and and goes over pre written code.  His quizzes and OLA instructions are full of typos and he is never clear in his instructions. He is worse than robert smith for sure.
Nice professor and gave average lectures. Reasonable OLAs. Tests weren't too difficult and took from projects, homework, and quizzes. Was a pretty lenient professor when it came to grading.

He was a great teacher for an introduction-level class! He didn't let how much he knew get in the way of dumbing it down for us. You do mostly labs during the lab time that seldom bleed into homework and then a handful of homework assignments. He WILL assign quizzes over the weekend and forget to mention it so check D2L and be aware of that!

Professor Al-Tobasei gave great advice to complete the assignments when they were given. The assignments felt like a fun puzzle. If you like a good challenge, this is the course for you. If you can do the assignments, the exams are a cake walk. The PwrPt contain most of the info to code, but the book was nice too.
His finals are very hard, and that is all it takes to determine whether you can take future classes.

Professor was really helpful, if I ever had a problem with something instead of telling me whats wrong he would lead me in the right direction of fixing the problem in the code, take advantage of the online powerpoints, and always check every weekend for quizzes

Java was not too bad. Group project was either a JavaFX or Android app. He will assign quizzes on d2l and may not necessarily tell you about them but it's good practice to expect a quiz or two on the weekend. Seemed to be a lenient grader with OLAs. Biggest advice: Tests are based on questions from the book. Might be helpful to *acquire* it

I took his C# class (CSCI 3037) offered in the Spring. The class consists of a group project, tests, quizzes, and homework open labs. He taught the class well and was very strict on attendance. His accent isn't the worst but it gets hard to understand sometimes when he starts to mumble, but he is willing to help and has an attitude sometimes.

If you have problems in this course go to his office hours! I had problems turning in CLA's and I went and he helped me fix my issue. This is the same for any assignment that you are struggling on or think is graded unfairly/wrong. Also check d2l over the weekend because he has a habit of just assigning things.

Awful professor I hate him. He is tough and his exams are stupid hard and a harsh grader. Doesnt give a damn about you. And tutoring never helps He told me to go to tutoring . I went to tutoring asking for help and told me if I dont understand what is it asking for they cant help me and kicked me out. He never responds to emails.
rating_tags: Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

review_text: Pretty decent tbh. Id say hes tough only bc of the labs which you can seek help from lab assistant. But other than that make sure you read the textbook chapters it will help A LOT. And before each test, which is only 2 not including final, practice your coding bc the coding questions are worth a lot of points! Overall really effective teacher
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

review_text: If you are going to be remote, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. We were half in class and half on Zoom, and if you were on Zoom, it's safe to say you weren't going to learn ANYTHING. The equipment that was provided to him was not good at all, and he did not know how to operate it well either. Save your money and WAIT.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: Pay attention in class and read through the chapters before hand. He is Lazy when doing final grading , does not own up to mistakes. Untruthful.

Dr Al-Tobasei is a nice guy, he's understanding and teaches well. I had some trouble in the last half of the semester/course but i think thats because that was right when we started to do online classes

Rafet was amazing, it's going to be near impossible to find a teacher as dedicated and inspirational as he was. I think it's safe to say that Dr. Tobasei changed my life. Not only did he teach Computer Science material during class, but he also taught us valuable life lessons. I've never met a more well-rounded and intelligent person. What a guy.
Tobasei is a swell guy. He makes occasional spelling errors but to say that his class has inspired me would be an understatement.

I took this class over zoom. Very easy class. One assignment per week on one of the Microsoft softwares, and they are very straightforward and easy. She even does them during class with you, so if you get a low grade on an assignment, it is not her fault. Would take her again!
rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

review_text: Professor Gu is great. The class is very easy, you are only given one assignment per week. Most of the assignments are facile tasks in Microsoft programs (excel, word, pp). The coding portion of the class can be difficult but her lectures give good instructions on how to complete them. Her test can be difficult but most do fine.

Dr. Gu is the best!!!! She is so nice and makes the class super easy (like she literally shows us how to do our homework step by step). There's only 1 assignment a week and a test every few weeks (the tests are really easy too). Definitely take her :)

I loved Dr. Gu! She covers a wide range of material in the computer orientation course. I even learned so much about topics I thought I was already proficient in. She thoroughly goes over all material and even shows you step by step how to do the labs. She often finished lectures early, but stayed on to help students with labs and homework. 10/10!

I took her online Computer Orientation class. Very nice. Attendance was mandatory. I love how she goes through the lab step by step explaining, so I could follow along. Not a lot of professors teach this way, and it is a big way that I learn. I appreciate it!

Such a nice lady! I wish we could have got to know her in person. If there's any confusion, she is always open to answering questions and explaining steps of the labs. She shows you exactly how to do the labs so it never felt like there was any homework. The only advice I'd give is to study for the closed AND open book tests.

There are 12 labs and 4 exams. She drops the lowest one of each. One day of the week she does a lecture style class and the other she actually does the entire lab.( I hardly attended the lectures and watched the videos the night before the lab). So as long as you follow her exact steps you will get an A on all the labs because she does them on vide

Super easy A! Professor Gu is extremely kind and explains everything in depth making it easy for you to understand. It's also all online so it was nice being able to just go grab a snack, etc. The only thing that might hinder some people is she does have a thick accent, but that wasn't an issue for me and I don't think it would be for many others.

All classes were on Zoom, so it allowed me to grab a bite to eat. Near the end we just delved into code which was mostly HTML and CSS, mostly was easy as she gave us resources. I'd recommend using Komodo Edit, as just using Notepad won't really help much.

She is very kind and the tests are very easy and open-book. There are a lot of labs but they are all fairly simple. She will drop the worst lab grade that you have, and the worst exam grade that you have, so if you made all As, you do not have to do the final lab or exam.

Yi Gu was absolutely amazing. The labs were super easy, and despite the class being on Zoom, she did an AMAZING job teaching the course and not making the class meetings boring. She definitely needs a raise because she cares about her students deeply.
She was super nice, understanding, and easy. We met once a week and she does the assignments with you during class. I hardly even listened and passed with an A.

Yi Gu's teaching gains a large amount of strength from her ability to find trouble spots in each of her students. Simply put, if Professor Yi Gu continues to place her best foot forward when giving valuable lessons here at MTSU, her students will forever do their best to mirror her image of passion, care, and overall effectiveness in the workplace.

This class is honestly really simple if you took any kind of computer class in high school. It's basically a class on how to use Excel and PowerPoint, with some extras. Dr. Gu did a good job at explaining the material. Tests were straightforward and all multiple choice. The final was the only one with short answer but she allowed a cheat sheet.

Overall a great teacher. She does have a bit of an accent, so listen carefully. She will answer any questions you have, so if you're confused actually show up to the labs (attendance not taken on lab days) If you do all the labs and review material for the tests, it's an easy A-B.

Very sweet woman. Just a little eccentric with her microphone box. Super thick accent and hard to understand. Can get off task easily. However, tests are simple if you study the guides given. Very easy A.
She's very kind, but has a little bit of a thick accent. Assignments are super straightforward and easy.

Dr. Phillips is a great professor but this class sucked the soul out of my life. There are just too many assignments and they can be difficult, especially of the OLA's. The exams are so long that you don't even have time to go back and check your work. 10 homeworks, 4 OLAs, 3 exams, final. You are expected to write a two page report for each OLA.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Test heavy

review_text: Exceptional teacher - one of the best I've ever had. This course was possibly the hardest CS class I've taken, but I learned way more than any other CS class too. The projects were super helpful - very clear grading criteria and organized course materials. Exams were similar to homework and everything was based on lecture material. Great class.
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: Undoubtedly the best professor in MTSU

I took AI in my last semester of college. 3rd hardest cs class I took, but I feel as though I learned A LOT. 4 projects, 3 tests, final, and 10 hw assignments is your grade. Each project you're required to write a minimum 2 page paper. Tests are based on lecture and HW (study assignments). Be careful with test 2. Grading is very thorough but clear
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Dr. Phillips is a very thorough instructor with a great and sometimes subtle sense of humor. Very patient with questions and prefers to ensure most everyone understands a concept before moving on. His classes aren't super easy, but that's because he mostly teaches higher-level courses, and he teaches them well. I quite enjoyed my classes with him.

Dr Phillips is widely considered the best professor in the department. He teaches some challenging classes, but he's fair and consistent in them and clearly communicates what you need to do to succeed, both in general and in his rubrics. You can expect him to give detailed lectures and to be knowledgeable about the topics. He is very supportive.

One of the best professors in the program. Very systematic and thorough in how he explains things, but still keeps it clear & simple. Doesn't have any noticeable attitude problems like other professors in the program. His class can be tough but you will learn alot. My only criticism is the projects can be confusing depending on your background.

Professor Phillips is my favorite Computer Science professor at MTSU. His 4350/5350 classes are undoubtedly difficult, likely more difficult than most 4000 level courses. However, he has clear expectations for projects and tests. If you are looking for an easy upper level class, this is not it. However, if you really want to learn, this is for you.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

review_text: A lot of work to do in his course. Every homework, project, exam are challenging, and they are not as he explains in the class.

Dr. Phillips is the worst professor in computer science. 4 projects. 10 homework. Difficult homework. 3 exams, every 6 chapters have a comprehensive exam about 15 pages. Final exam. Include 3 exam+homework+projects. He forced us to come and made us think that this was the university's decision.

Dr. Phillips explains difficult concepts very well and makes them relatively easy. He is very well organized and has clear grading criteria. If you take the 3130 class, my biggest suggestion is don't fall behind because all of the labs are used for the final project. As long as you can keep up with the labs, I highly recommend Dr. Phillips.

Dr. phillips instantly became my favorite cs professor and in my top 5 for all. He has great lectures, if you pay attention in class his test become much easier, and building a Simple computer in his class sounds daunting but he supplies great tutorials to follow to be able to do them!
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

review_text: I Dr. Phillips for CSCI3130 and was very excited to have him for CSCI4350 and was not disappointed. I highly recommend Phillips for any course.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

review_text: Dr. Phillips is my favorite teacher so far. He really does know his stuff and will take the time to explain anything to you very well. His lectures make sense, and he explains the detail behind concepts instead of skipping over them. Understand the course may be difficult at times, however with him you will succeed if you put in the effort.

Dr Phillips is a fantastic professor. His class is very organized, and he is exceptional at explaining difficult concepts. His tests are reasonable in difficulty and length, and he also gives partial credit. Take him if you have the chance!
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Phillips was a fantastic professor. He has a a very good tone, speed, and volume to his voice during his lectures and his explanations of course content were well-articulated. The labs for the class were quite challenging at times, though. Overall I would say that he was a stellar professor and look forward to taking his A.I. class.

**** THIS REVIEW IS FOR COSC 486 (unsure how to add courses)  Class was difficult gave only exams as major grade calculation. Exams were also on paper written no tech and no calculator allowed. review_text: He is one of the best professors you will ever encounter, he is beyond caring and investing his time into making sure he teaches you well regarding the topics. He teaches in many different forms so everyone understands the topics and his assignments are well designed for you to think. If you can take him!
Take his classes if you want to learn the subject really well
He helped me understand this alot, me personally I would take him again and he is an amazing teacher.
He is amazing, I highly recommend him because he helps you out alot and is kind.

take this class if you are looking to pass and not learn anything. he doesn't grade anything, once you submit that's an automatic 100% so no room for feedback. also, he can be very condensed and loves to argue with students
Very lazy teachers, doesnt teach just cancel classes to work in problems with no explanation

He gave a final exam that was a take home that took a total of 18 hours and I had 3 days to do it. The amount of time for the work he gave was unprecedented, and he never actually graded labs. They were always a 100% when I did not submit sections. There was no clear feedback and his notes were a PowerPoint slide that was illegible.
He is ok at explaining but he is very sarcastic and he is an absolute heartless grader.
He really explains in detail, his assignments and projects are fair, but they will take up your time, so be sure to start working on them early enough inorder to meet the due date.

Sontag is by no means a perfect professor. However, he definitely knows his stuff when it comes to the topics in this course. rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: The course has a lot of material and is hard to follow unless you only have one course to manage. Only the initial lectures are good but you will loose track very soon. The homeworks are extremely challenging and take a lot of time. To earn a respectable grade is extremely tough. If you are tight on gpa requirement, I dont recommend taking it.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: Great professor. The course is pretty hard, but the topics covered are cutting edge. I recommend it if you like math and would like to deepen your knowledge in probabilistic inference and advanced machine learning techniques.
rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: He is one of the smartest guy I have ever met. He thinks fast, talks fast (but very clear) and is very passionate about what he is teaching, which encourages you to keep up with him and want to know more. The course materials are well planned, it covers the basic graphical model stuff.
not recommend to future students. The course is tough and hard and the professor cannot make it understandable.
boring professor, very hard homework problems and some even undoable.

This guy still participated in mapping out the terrible syllabus even if he is not teaching the machine learning course. The instructor is his classmate and exactly a weird guy to avoid. do not take the course.

Can you imagine 26 homework programming problems of machine learning to be done in one week? if you do not want to get a very low grade, do not take his course. For students with data science major, i think you should copy some answers from other students. do not waste too much time on his course.

The materials are hard and his teaching is even worse than any other professors. In this case, you have to learn by yourself and spend almost all your spare time on this course. The bad thing is that Sontag always assumes everyone only register 1 coursesemester (i.e. his course). If you can get a B, then you are in the top 5% of students.

I work very hard for this course, 8+ hours per day and 6+ days per week. However, I got C- finally. In my opinion, a good course should be the course which could let students who are cleaver get A, who work very hard get B, who never cost time on it get F and others get C and D. But for this course, only very hard working clever students may get A

Don't take this class if you are looking for something easy or something to fulfill some requirements and don't take this class if you aren't willing to work hard.   If you are interested in the material and put in the hours, the class is quite rewarding and covers a large portion of the relevant material in probabilistic graphical models.
The material is difficult. But if you go to office hours, start homeworks early, it's doable.   Easily one of the best professors I've ever had.

Professor Sontag may assign a lot of homework and readings, but you actually walk away with a clear picture of the material.  His lectures are easily the best I've attended in Courant.  He seems to genuinely care about students in his classes.

Can you imagine that a professor can make more than 15 out of 40 drop the course? Sontag is such a guy. His slides and homework are copied from other institutions since he knows nothing about the course materials. The NYU curriculum committee checked his course after final and he is now in a very embarassing situation. He may lose his position soon

To be honest, it's not an easy course, but a challenge, speaking from a CS background student.  The professor is smart and helpful, so you can definitely learn a lot from him. It'll be better that you have some math background before taking this course.

Sontag thought he knew everything, but truth is....I cannot remember how many times he got stuck on the platform doing math derivations because this embarrassing moment happens all the time. Anyway, he seemed to enjoy his frequent interactions with the front-row noisy noisy girl, which makes this course a must if you want to ruin your college life.

He is, unfortunately, too fond of his own inventions and usually makes things more complicated than they need be. I wish the homework wasn't presented in terms of Sontags confusing non-standard formulations. You will have to do a loooooooot of homework to get even a B. An A if you were a computer working 247 at the library.

Tough instructor. Never recommend to future students. He was always confident with his hard homework but actually his homework was copied from other universities, such as Stanford and UMass. He is also a weird guy since he enjoys contradicting to himself. Cannot understand why this guy can be hired by NYU Courant.

Tons of homeworks with little instructions, and the scope is way beyond what is covered in lecture notes. Not recommended unless you take only with course during the semester and devote all your leisure time on it, which is almost impossible for most students. Spend time on something else more rewarding.

Is David Sontag good at research? Absolutely not! He even does not know how to do research. Check his website and clearly see that he holds the final place of almost all the publications. He has to learn the materials himself, even from his students. This is the reason why students cannot understand his class, waste time and money.

Bad experience. As a lecturer, he did not know how to moderate the class. It is not uncommon that a noisy girl in the front row saying a lot of nonsense during lecture hours. Letting this happen in all his lectures is a clear sign of lacking control of the class, which left a bad impression on other students.
One of the best professors ever. Clearly cares about his students learning. Be prepared to do lots of work.

The professor is very passionate about course topics, give lectures clearly. Also David is very good at research, check google scholar for his publication list, it's really outstanding among his peers. But the course is difficult because of the materials and topics. Prepare to do a lot of mathematical derivation, and start homework early.

I strongly agree with above comments. Yes, he got his PhD in 2009 and he is still an assistant professor now so you can think about his ability. He made many mistakes in class and his mathematical induction skills are extremely poor. There were initially 41 students enrolled in the course, but now less than 20 students are still there.

For those who think it is easy, I guess it means you have to spend all your free time in this course alone, without looking for a job  doing research with other professors. A piece of advice, David Sontag has stayed as an assistant professor for an extremely long time, with little possibility of getting a tenure. Little hope doing research for him

Just genuinely a great educator, I get excited whenever I fill out the schedule and seeing his name on the class I am taking.
Prof. Unan is one of the best! His classes are engaging and he genuinely wants us to succeed. The lectures are aligned with the free textbook, and unstuck ai was super helpful for prep.
Unan is one of the best professors of my entire college life
Truly one of my favorite professors. He's very passionate about teaching and starts to prepare you for real-world application. The coding projects are fun too. Tests are mainly on lecture material.
baller class, lots of opportunities for bonus points and extra credit. only critique is that sometimes he steps a little to far away from the mic when lecturing.
One of the best classes I have ever taken

I've taken CS 103, 203, 303, 330, and 332 with Dr. Unan. He is great and gives the most fair assignments and exams in the CS department. If you want a good professor whose classes teach you how to program, easy to talk to, and will help you, take Dr. Unan.

installing so many libraries/dependencies might be annoying, however Unan's clear and engaging teaching style, and up to date course content made learning web development a joyful experience. Creating a successful final project app instilled a sense of self-assurance in me
I believe it is the best Unan course. The homework are archaic, must be updated asap

Dr. Mahmat's instruction during my first semester was exceptional and greatly contributed to my academic progress. His dedication to teaching and expertise in the field of computer science have been invaluable to me. I am excited to continue learning from him in their upcoming web development course over the summer.
I have taken 5 classes with Unan so far, he is a true legend in the department
Dr. Unan is one of my favorite professors, I suggest that you take his classes whenever possible.
Unan is an outstanding professor who truly wants his students to succeed.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Completing the final project in this class was instrumental in helping me land a job. Regarding Unan's class, I would suggest he improve the quality of his Teaching Assistants. While he has had great TAs in his other courses, the graduate student TA in this class was subpar.
Unan is doing a marvelous job again. His Teaching Assistants were great up to this semester, he should not hire one of them again (VM)

Unan is a stellar professor. His lectures, exams, and assignments are easy and fun. John is an excellent and intelligent teaching assistant who cares about his students and helps tremendously. The secondary TA, Dylan, is quite rude and unhelpful, and I've seen him show distaste in many interactions with students. Go to John for you questions!
One of the cool classes of UAB Computer Science. Dr. Mahemaut is a fantastic person

Unan is a great professor. You can miss several lectures, but you should go to class. Lectures are all recorded and posted. There are a few homework assignments, but they are very easy. You can go to TA office hours for any help. Labs are easy grades as well. Midterm and final are based on concepts as opposed to coding.
It was an amazing class. Best professor and Teaching assistants ever

One of the best professors Ive taken at UAB. His lectures were interesting. He was able to relate abstract ideas with real world examples easily. He listens to his students and goes out of his way to make sure everyone understands the material before tests/homework is due. I will definitely take him again.

I have prior coding experience and I think this class and Unan did a fantastic job of introducing CS. Very knowledgeable and helpful. The lab portion felt to me like forced office hours with TAs, but I know it was useful for some who have little to no experience. Pay attention in the lectures andreach out tothe TAs if you don'tunderstand anything!
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

review_text: He is one of the coolest professors at UAB CS department. He knows his stuff and he is an excellent guy personality-wise. You will learn a million in this class and it will help you to crack the coding interviews (not enough though). His exams are pretty much similar every semester, so pay attention to the sample exam.
Unan is my favorite professor at UAB. The only challenge for me was the class size and a chunk of grad students who tries to cheat in the exam. The linux crash course should be introduced earlier.

Good person that cares about students. He's willing and available to help, but also let students learn on their own. The lectures are a little long and hard to pay attention to because it's so much information, but he doesn't add any fluff to his lectures or go off topic too much. Tests are average difficulty, labs/homework are easy.

Had Unan for 103 and 203 w/ no prior coding experience. He is kind and reasonable, but you'll likely learn more from other resources. Lectures are simply okay; slides are VERY info-heavy but helpful. Exams are super easy. (Read the slides.) Labs and HW are doable; start early and ask for help. Heads up: a lot of the previous 5/5 reviews are fake.
I have learned a ton of C coding in this class. Unan simplifies the topics for everyone. He cares about the students and provides any help that we need.

It was my favorite class last semester. Dr. Mahmut is a fantastic professor,  he makes you interested in the subject. I will never forget his tower of stack game and his experiment with rice and chess board :)
As always, Unan did a great job this semester. He is willing to teach you as long as you don't cheat

Although this class is not the most interesting he makes it interesting. There are 6 HW. if you are not a great test taker be sure to get as many points on the HW's sometimes there will be bonus points so get as many points as you can. In most cases if your grade on the final is greater than one of your midterms he will replace it with your lowest.
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

review_text: Dr. Unan is very patient and does a great job of describing hard material. Homeworks are worth a big chunk of your grade, so I'd suggest starting on them as early as possible. The TAs that correspond with lab are great resources to go to when struggling on assignments. Attendance is important, as the material covered in class are on hws and exams.

Extremely chill professor. If you have any experience with coding and you listen to lectures, this class will be extremely easy for you. He is so generous with the amount of time given for assignments and lets you turn in hws late. Exams are extremely easy and there's only two. HWs make up more of your grade than the exam though

If you pay attention in class and do the homework you should ace the exams. He explains concepts and provides examples for those concepts which are applicable to homework and exams. It is plain to see he cares about the students and wants everyone to have a good experience (welcomes questions and feedback). I wish all my teachers were like Unan.
Very helpful lectures and homework. Several resources to help you throughout your work, and very thorough in demonstrations and examples.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Dr. Unan is a wonderful professor who is great at teaching!!! His homework and overall assignments are very clear and you will get an A in the class if you use the resources provided to their full potential. Slides are provided 10min before lecture, so you can look at those instead of getting the textbook. The exams are multiple choice and fair.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Unan is a great professor who is very helpful and forgiving. His lectures are always well done and engaging. Easily the best professor I've had at UAB.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: Phenomenal professor. Lectures are very clear and understandable, and homework is based directly on information covered in class. By far the best professor I have had at UAB.
Even though It is a challenging course, the professor and the Teaching Assistants are fantastic. They know how to teach and they are eager to help in any case. Loved it

Hes a very knowledgeable man but a horrible professor. He reads directly off the slides and gives random examples that are off topic. Unfortunately you dont have another choice, you have to take him for this course and others. The labs were a joke and the TAs didnt help. They only showed off. If you have any coding experience you should be fine.
I took 4 classes (103,203,330,332) from Unan and he is such a fantastic professor. I owe him my career
He is a good professor
I wish he teaches more graduate courses such as machine learning or other data science related courses.
I have taken 103,203, and 330 with unan and they were all exceptional courses
A tough course to understand at the beginning, later on each single topics makes sense. Professor is extremely helpful and nice!!!
Professor Unan is my favorite professor by far!!!

Dr. Unan is hands-down my favorite professor I have had to date. He is very knowledgeable and helpful, and he regularly goes out of his way to make sure that everyone is clear on the material. He is incredibly understanding (probably more than he should be) and offered every opportunity to make up for mistakes He's a must-pick if you are a CS major
Another awesome CS course with Unan!!! I recommend you to practice C coding a little bit prior to this course

10 assignments worth 50%/two midterms worth 20%/attendance 10%/final exam 20%. Just make sure to have some prior knowledge in C and you should be good for this course. When starting assembly, it's important to attend those lab lectures as they really help. The exams in this course are lecture heavy, and there is no need for a textbook to pass.
Object-oriented was a nightmare first, after you get it, it became a piece of cake. Professor Unan and his assistants were quite helpful. I love UAB CS, go blazers !!!

It was the hardest course I had ever taken. I enjoyed the lectures and learned awful lot of stuff, however the homework-lab work was quite challenging. Dr. Mahmut is an impressive teacher and his assistance are so helpful
great prof
Just like the other "Unan Courses", I learned a million in 203.

At first, I was so dissapointed with the theory lectures and the topics did not make sense. Once we start applying the knowledge, I understand and appreciate the OOP concept. Dr. Mahmut is pretty helpful and he truly cares about you learning the material as well

Professor unan is an invaluable member of the UAB CS dept. His lectures are super interesting and hilarious. True gem. Show up to class, do the work, you will lear a ton. TAKE HIM, YOU WILL NOT REGRET IT !!!!

At the beginning of the semester, I had pretty hard to time with the JAVa programming, later on Dr. Unan helped me a lot and I was able to figure it out. One of the most helpful and knowledgeable person at UAB

Great instructor and Empathetic person. He does have a passion about what he teaches and gives concrete examples. He is not giving challenging homeworks due to the extremely low level of UAB students. One of the Best UAB professors.
Great course to learn Web App development, the course mainly focus on the server side applications. The professor is a huge help as always. It is a great experience to develop a useful website
Spectacular course, you will learn a lot. Cant wait to take cs203

review_text: Assembly programming was a nightmare for many of us, however; professor and the teaching assistants were extremely helpful. You will learn a lot!!!

Awesome professor&course!!!

clear and precise lectures, knowledgeable professor. I suggest you to apply for an internship after you take this course, the professor will be giving a lot of hints about the internship/job interview questions

The most challenging course in my life, but it was a great experience. I was working 20 hours per week for this course. I have learned a million, now I enjoy solving coding problems. Unan is an adorable professor and the Teaching Assistants are spectacular.

challenging class, but you will learn deeply
I have taken CS103 and CS203 with unan. He knows what he is teaching and how to teach

review_text: To pass the midterm and final, go over lecture notes. To make As in homework and lab, you'll have to go to office hours and ask questions. great professor, the best college course ever. The grading criteria is not crystal clear though :(

I was expecting a boring theory class at beginning, clearly unan knows how to teach. He is emphasizing the important points again and again. He is trying his best to teach what we will be using as a software developer. Assembly part is pretty challenging, however; the TA and professor helps a lot

The best instructor ever. He motivates the students all the time (the short motivational videos at the beginning of each lecture was priceless for me). review_text: Dr. Unan is very straightforward in his approach to teaching the material. It may be dry at times, but his slides are amazingly crafted so it is very easy to review. rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: The course itself is a little bit hard, but the professor and the assistants are spectacular. I truly enjoy his classes in this department. He is a very helpful professor and he is good at teaching. Also many thanks to Greg and Johnny. I learned a lot from all of you guys

I took this class two semesters ago and today I got my first job interview. Most questions came from this course and surprisingly I could remember almost all of them. Unan knows how to teach and he is good at giving real life examples. He does have a passion about what he teaches and gives concrete examples.I would HIGHLY recommend him.
great professor, the guy knows what he is teaching

It was the hardest course for me, because I had no experience with computers at all. I had to work really hard to understand the material and get an A from the homeworks and labs. review_text: One of the hardest intro course I have ever seen/heard in my lifetime. However, I can say that it worth every second I spent for this course. You will love CS and learn a lot

Dr Unan is truly one of the best I've had in my college career. He is very concerned with the success of students and very respected. He is always accessible both through email and outside of class. He is always giving the tips about interview questions or real life examples. I can't believe that I got almost similar questions in my last interview

This is one of the most crucial courses in CS and it is super comprehensive. The 2. programming course and you are learning a new language an a new concept. It is pretty challenging course but Dr. Unan helps a lot. His lectures have everything you need and he is well organized. Do not miss any lecture because he is giving lot of hints about hw

The professor is super helpful and knowledgeable. This is a mandatory core course, you can't skip it, so try to take it from professor unan
The most important class in the department. Unan is an amazing professor, he is explaining all major topics in a clear manner. He understands the students and provides the best possible help

review_text: Great professor. Very engaging lectures and great at breaking down concepts. Provides students with extra resources outside of class to better understand the material and an overall professor that is for the students.

7 homeworks due bi-weekly/13 labs due weekly/1 midterm/1 final. Teacher is understandable but has a slight accent. Dont need the book and lectures are all you need. review_text: Professor Peng is very knowledgeable and passionate about his teaching. He's always nice and patient when students ask questions. On the final presentation day, he even prepared snacks for us, which is very nice. The course materials are well organized and easy to follow. I would say it's a wonderful experience taking his course!

Professor Peng's expectations are high and the material is complex. However, he is always available to answer questions after class and is accommodating for meetings outside of office hours. Be prepared to spend a significant amount of time on homework and researching the course material, especially if this is your first intensive ML class.

The course lectures are up-to-date reflecting current ML practices and research. I had the opportunity to learn ML concepts ranging from beginner to advanced levels. This comprehensive content helps you build a solid foundation while progressively addressing more complex topics with hands-on homework assignments.
I understood well in class. Learning with real life example was fun. He interacts as well in class and very nicely refreshes your previous lectures along the way.

I would have liked the classes to have been clearer. While the homework was beneficial, it often felt disconnected from the material covered in class, requiring additional effort to complete. Clarity regarding the expectations for the final project would have been appreciated.

I took his graduate ML class. Dr. Peng is very concerned that we should have a deep understanding of core machine learning concepts rather than regurgitate all the small details. As a graduate course, I also appreciated that Dr. Peng focused on relevant topics like GANs and meta-learning that are still receiving a lot of attention in ML research.

Most of his classes were going over slides that were prepared either by TA or some other professors. There were no exams at all and the final grade was based on just homework and the final project. The final project included presentation, reporting, and peer reviewing. Enjoyed the class with interesting topics but will not take this course again.

Grade is based only on 3 exams, which feels unfair and too high-stakes. Most grading was delegated to a TA whose standards were extremely strict with unclear/limited partial credit. Around 20% of the class failed. I strongly suggest you run away
