review_text: Absolutely fantastic. Favorite professor and class at KU. Super engaging, detailed, and helpful. I learned a ton, but never felt out of my depth.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Very engaging. It is obvious how much work he puts into his classes and that he wants students to succeed.

Professor Davidson is nice and funny, but he needs to explain some concepts concisely. He gives 8 projects and 4 quizzes, which are hard. Labs, check-ins, and written works are easy since you get full credit if you attend his lectures. If you work on the assignments punctually and have a good project partner, then you will do well on this course.

The material is hard, but he delivers it with passion and puts a bunch of jokes on the slides Grading is quite lenient and he resonates with students very well Professor asks for feedback several times though the semester and you can immediately see him taking in the advice received There are optional projects available (which are very rewarding)

This class will take up more time than all your other classes. Projects are very hard, make sure you have a good partner. Quiz grading was fair, he curves them a lot which is nice. He has his grading and class structure very well done. Many grades so you won't be done over one bad assignment. Class is hard but you'll prob get a B if you're > avg.
rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

review_text: If you dont enjoy working on class work for hours every day this class may not be for you. The amount of work is absurd, and not even beneficial to your learning. Most of the class is very lost and usually end up learning from each other instead of the lectures.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: This professor is truly an anomaly. A friendly, patient, and a professor that truly cares for his craft and students. He makes a difficult class easy to understand. He is willing to work with his students and will constantly make adjustments in your favor. However, there is a large workload and the class is still difficult but you'll be fine.

He is epic. Actually a good professor who shows passion for what he teaches. He indirectly encourages students to learn instead of trying to regurgitate information, Tests are very fair and he makes sure you are properly prepared. Literally nothing bad to say.

Great professor. One of the best professors ive had at KU. He cares about his students and make sure they learn the materials well. 665 is a tough course with a lot of project and lab. But you will learn a lot.

Drew Davidson is a phenomenal professor. He truly cares about how all of his students are doing and caters to every level of ability. His lectures are very engaging as he clearly puts forth lots of effort into the design of the course content. He always sticks around after class for student questions. This class is a lot of work, but it's worth it!

Hands down probably the best professor in the department. He's very organized and tells you exactly what you need to learn and when you need to learn it by. The way he teaches is also very nicely paced, especially for a topic as heavy as compilers. There is a lot of work though, but he gives ample time to complete them. He is also VERY responsive.

Class was challenging but rewarding. Dr. Davidson puts more effort into course quality and resources for students than any other professor I've had at KU. You'll learn so much in his class if you are willing to put in the work.
Lectures are semi mandatory. Have to do an assignment and turn it in if you dont attend. Good class though. Projects feel well balanced
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: He's easily one of the best professors I've ever had. He makes you work hard but never without good reason and he's always there to help if you need him. He's constantly going above and beyond for his students and always asking for feedback on how he can do better. I promise you will not regret taking a class with Drew.

Dr. Davidson is an absolutely fantastic professor. He is incredibly knowledgeable, caring, and goes way above and beyond for his students. He is without a doubt my favorite professor at KU and I plan on taking any class I can that is taught by him. 11/10, three thumbs up, absolutely fantastic. KU needs to tenure him immediately.

Drew really cares. He holds extra review sessions, he buys pizza for his students, he'll even do extra video lectures upon request. In my four years of college I haven't met a professor doing more for their students than Drew. And most importantly, he's willing to listen to feedback and adapt to make the course better. Would take again.

Im certain all those reviews telling you to disregard the previous ones are from himself. He's lukewarm when it comes to actually teaching, basically just reads off slides. The quizzes have little to do with the slides themselves. You're better off just reading the textbook and watching the author's own videos on youtube. He never answers emails.

Good professor, interesting teaching style. I think he is alright, nothing good nothing bad I would say the reviews before the exams are helpful, class is made harder than it needs to be. The topic is very interesting, he can tweak some things and be greater.

Fav EECS prof. Always greeted me with a smile when I had questions after class. Super clear lecturing & always asks if we have questions (doesn't make you feel dumb at all). Sends out announcements/reminders for quizzes. He very clearly wants everyone to do well. 3 exams - 20%, 20%, 25%. iClicker attendance - but not graded for it.
Not sure about the red reviews, but he gives great lectures and homework with quizzes for understanding. If you study, you should do fine

I don't understand those bad review. This is a wonderful professor who is cared support students' success. He made communication networks become very interesting. If you go to class and do homework then you will be fine. Plus, the WireShark labs are very interesting and helpful for understanding the concept of this class

Be prepared to learn the material independently, as this professor shows little concern for their students. While the course material is interesting, the professor approach is disappointing. He doesn't even take the time to prepare a proper syllabus. It's unfortunate that this is a mandatory course and that he is the only professor teaching it.

I don't understand all these bad reviews! He's respectful to all his students and his lectures are clear. Go to class, only about 20% of students actually show up & I bet those are the people leaving reviews
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

review_text: Previous ratings of this professor are outdated. The lectures are extremely engaging. The homeworks provide good background knowledge on the material learned in class. The tests and quizzes are more than fair. One of the top courses I have taken so far at KU.

This professor provides very interesting and engaging lectures, there are weekly homework and quizzes, however they are not too difficult. Some of the homework asks you to learn new content on your own that he has not and will not cover in class.  Overall and easy going professor.
Attendance is expected, but be ready to learn everything by yourself. Homeworks are regular. Aweful teacher but content is interesting.

I am glad to see others had a similar experience with Dr. Hashemi. He was quite unpleasant and made me less interested in the material, although I enjoyed the content. Take this course if there is another professor, but if it is only Hashemi, I would recommend waiting or learning the material on your own.

The most difficult and uncaring professor I have had at KU. He has NO interest in the wellbeing of students and whether or not they are learning, rather he is simply condescending and tries to impose his knowledge on you at any moment he can. Extremely, extremely tough grader. I would not take a course by him, ever again.
I don't think he really cares about the students. The test and homework questions are ambiguous. It was hard to know what he actually wants.

Professor Hashemi will not force you to come to class, but will call you out if he even thinks you aren't paying attention. He maintains a distinct lack of ability to lecture, and teaches this class to only let him research at KU. He cannot write test questions, homework questions, or effectively receive student feedback. The worst professor I had.

One of the worst professors I've ever had. The average and median on the first exam was a very low D, though a curve was not considered for that exam and the professor even went so far as to say the exam was actually "easy." Lectures and homework questions are often unclear and ambiguous.
Just awesome. "my fingers never leave my hands" "It's turtles all the way down"  He will remove the mystery from computer science, If you ask questions you will get great answers.

Hardest grader ever, will assign problems on stuff you covered in class for a whole 5 minutes. Doesn't sympathize if you know what you're doing, only if you've memorized what he's said, though you can easily get an A if you know what you're doing.
Very entertaining and fun. Explains thing clearly, brings a lot of examples, asks questions  and respects your opinion. Laid back and always willing to help. As a lecturer, he is pretty good.
His lectures were fairly boring, but he was a cool guy. He was easy to talk to and get help from. Sometimes he could be a little bit too casual, as in walking around class barefooted.

Explains the material very well for both beginner coders and more experienced students. He clearly still codes a lot (unlike some older professors). The class itself is difficult if you're new to object-oriented programming, but Kukla is very helpful if you ask questions. Bonus points for funny and interesting tangents during lecture.
Very cool and funny guy! Explains things clearly, always willing to help. He's very understanding and encouraging, which is really nice especially if you're an amateur programmer.

I took CMPE650 with professor Mohsenin. She is one of the best professor I've ever had. Her teaching style and lectures are great. All the homework prepared me for a very interesting final project which was designing a solution for a real-world problem and I learnt a lot from doing it. I absolutely recommend taking this course with her.

Professor Mohsenin is undoubtedly one of the most outstanding instructors I have encountered in my entire graduate program. Her teaching approach is stimulating and engaging, and the courses she delivers are consistently current and applicable. The final project that she assigned to us for course 650 was very enjoyable and educational.

I was actually looking forward to 415. One of the things I appreciated most about the course was the challenging and thought-provoking projects. They were designed to push us to think critically and creatively, and I learned a lot from them. I also recommend attending the lectures, as she covers a lot of important topics.

CMPE 650 Course taught me how to design a CNN accelarator in verilog for it's course project. The Professor was very helpful clearing all of my doubts and at last with the discussion with her, I could finally complete my project and design a CNN accelarator which is great. I would like to advice anyone who is willing to take her course, go for it.

I had a great time in 650 with Tinoosh. The project and the assignments were really very interesting. I would recommend that you start them sooner as they would require some significant amount of thought and effort.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Accessible outside class

review_text: She waists your time. Avoid her.
Forced to take her for some courses. Very unorganized and all over the place.
She is actually the most unorganized teacher I have ever had. She does not know what's going on half of the time.

really helpful and if you are interested will make this course the joy of the semester. Grading is good. the projects are really helpful to get good grip over FPGA. if you dont have any prior background in verilog, dont worry she would make everything clear. pay attention,give time and this would be best CMPE course ever.

Good professor. Pop-quizzes & Lectures will help you prepare for exams. Write quickly during the exams, you might run out of time. Fair grader. Projects can take up a lot of time so start when they are assigned. She is a very interactive teacher so you won't fall asleep and she is very approachable if you have questions during office hours.

He is a very good teacher. He writes everything on the board so you have to take notes, but he goes slowly and explains everything thoroughly. Homework assignments were short and usually very easy. Overall an easy A if you go to class.

Dr. Farhat is an awesome professor! There is no textbook and everything is written on the board. He also repeats everything that he written. His accent is a little hard to understand at first, but gets easier as time goes on. The class is all memorization and an easy A. He doesn't want anyone to fail and tries everything to make everyone succeed.
Farhat is easily one of my favorite teachers that I have had.  Good lecturer, provides plenty of notes and examples, always eager to answer questions.
No Comments

CIST 1010 was a decent class that made me aware of campus resources I otherwise wouldn't have known about. Attendance is mandatory.   Dr. Dorn is pretty friendly, and makes his expectations clear. He is willing to help you outside of class.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

review_text: This course is meant to be an light intro to college and Professor Dorn takes it way to seriously. He grades assignments toughly when they should be graded for completion. This is generally an ok class but Professor Dorn makes it harder than it needs to be.

He does basically nothing all semester. He was too lazy to grade anything until a week after the semester ended so you had no idea how you were doing. Then when he finally grades your assignments, he does it extremely harsh for things that should just be busy work. Definitely has a superiority complex.

I definitely learned Java, that's for sure. Class operates like a 5 credit course. I didn't know a thing about programming before taking Intro to CS1 and this ramps it up to a new level. If you have a job/many non-college responsibilities take this class with easy classes to keep yourself sane. Because his course piles on an ungodly amount of work.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

review_text: The lectures help very little, homework takes an ungodly amount of time and what you're supposed to do is vague. This class is a freshman level class and it is treated like it is a graduate level. This class is 3 credits, lab mandatory, with the lab being 0 credits. Honestly, change majors if you have to take this class. It's not worth it.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: Time expectations and work load felt unrealistic for 3 cr hr. Semester felt like immersion experience, but numerous resources were made available to help students stay afloat. Lectures were mostly engaging. Dorn is bright, enthusiastic, and eager to help students succeed. I feel I would have learned more if not so stressed the entire time, though.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: Dorn expects students to spend 15+ hours working on Java II outside the classroom. The class is extremely difficult and time consuming. If you're not so knowledgeable about Java then you'll have to work extra hard just to stay afloat because there is no lack of work. Avoid if possible, but it's usually required for IS&T majors.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: The course material is easier than previous semester. He would love to help you solve the problem. If you do assignment earlier every time, you will feel better.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

review_text: If he taught Graduate level or Senior level class he would be perfect. However, it is Freshman level and is ridiculously time consuming. The amount of time you will spend on his java 2 class for the meager 3 credits is not equitable. Avoid if possible.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

review_text: Coming from a high school java class was a huge jump to the level of java II. It was a very time intensive class, however Dorn gave plenty of examples and assistance that helped me a lot. There are a lot of specific rules and tough concepts that java has, however Dorn will give plenty of activities and examples in lecture that help a ton.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

review_text: Java II is a hard class, but Dorn was a very good lecturer and made class interesting. If you work on the class a little each day, it honestly won't take up too much of your time, and there are a lot of resources out there to help you succeed in the class. I think UNO should make this a 5 credit class for the amount of work required, however.
rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: Java 2 is definitely a "weed out" class. He expects a lot from you. Honestly, way too much. Assignments are very vague and exam averages are low (60%). If you are a below-average to average student, you won't pass. Students who had trouble with Java I will have to go above and beyond. Textbook isn't required. Expect a lot of time with this class.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: Java 2 is a hard class regardless of who teaches it, and I think Dr. Dorn does a pretty decent job at being accessible and doing the best to answer questions presented to him. He shines in the lectures and I personally really like his approach to them. Textbook is useful but not "required", and expect a lot of time spent on homework.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: The hardest class I have ever taken. All HW is graded by an auto-grader. It is very bad at telling you what you did wrong and will give 0's even though code matches test cases and is working. A lot of work each week as well. Some weeks I spent around 40 hours just trying to not fall behind. Instructor was also not very responsive to questions.

My class grade right now is a 91% with every category at a 98% and above besides my 60% exam grade. I feel like many students struggle in this class. The class average is around a 75% from that being said. HUGE advice is to start on your homework assignments the day they come out and DONT partner up on hw assignments. You won't pass if ur average.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: Dorn is the head of the Java department at UNO, and Java 2 is one of the hardest classes I have ever taken. I am a senior and this freshman level, intro class is harder than my senior level classes. He expects students to spend AT LEAST 15 hours a week on just his class. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

Avoid this very poor educator. Dr. Dorn has no respect for his students' time (according to the syllabus, he expects students to spend 15+ hrs/wk outside class on this course - imagine if every teacher designed 3 credit hour courses this way). Class time was worthless, you have to watch video lectures for credit anyways so take the online class.

This instructor tries to be funny sometimes during the class, but one it comes to grade your work he would be someone else. Expect to get F at the end of semester for no reasons. The class is very tough. He's the second worst teacher I've ever had. Expect to spend entire week on one assignment, and then get %50 because of the hidden test cases.

Avoid taking this class with Brian Dorn. Avoid taking any class with him. If you a weak, he will take advantage of you and fail you to offset the budget so you will have to take it again. This man is by far the most terrible teacher I have ever had. He plays favorites and thinks he is the god of all computer related information.

Get ready to spend your entire semester on one class. Worst teacher I have ever had. He was my instructor for Java II. This guy doesn't even realize his code doesn't work in assignments until 2 days before the due date. He is very disrespectful to students and carries a haughty attitude.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: Java 2 at UNO is pretty tough anyway without the professors destroying all of your hard work. You'll spend hours and hours on an assignment only to get a 50% on the assignments because of "hidden test cases". The other teacher isn't any better. If you have to take it, make this the only tough class this semester and go to the learning center.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Tests are tough

review_text: Does a great job engaging students during class, but does not write reasonable tests by any means. Expect the average test scores of the class to be in the 60s. Make sure you work on getting good scores for your homework and participation grades, because your test grades won't be great.

Dorn does a great job engaging his students during lectures which is his strength.  He encourages participation and it helps if you are interested in the material.  The homework and tests are graded relatively tough.  Expect to be able to explain your thoughts clearly and concisely.  Explaining in generalities does not work.

Overall Dorn is a decent teacher.  Yes, there is a significant workload, so be warned.  Lots of time-consuming assigned readings, many of which weren't that helpful.  He could be clearer with his expectations on assignments and how he grades.  Several very time-consuming group projects.  He knows the material well and is willing to help, though.

Tough only in regards to the amount of work. The content is interesting and it's manageable.  When the entire class has problems understanding something, he will take the time to address questions thoroughly, especially before exams.
The worst professor i have  ever had.
Class is fun. But a lot of work. 4 exams (really?). Too many assignments, projects and exams ruin the entertaining part of the class.

Yeah, I agree there is a lot of work to do, but I consider them very valuable, I did learn a lot in this course and it's very useful in the future. But I do think for the current course load, this should be a 6 credit class.

There is a lot of work in his class but most of it is kind of fun.  He is definitely comically overbearing, but you get used to it.  I have no idea how he grades the tests.  I did horrible on one and alright on another.  If you're not intimdated by him, he is really good at answering questions.  He is a really good teacher.

Very very heavy course load. AVOID! 30+ pages of reading before every class. 7+ project sized "lab" assignments each due within 1 week. 3 long "short-answer" exams.  1 big sized project. 1 research project(Grad Only). Class participation credit. Curve given only by redoing exam essay question at home. High maintenance, do not use 100 point scale

Ricks designed the course with a lecture and sprint day each week. He focused 50% on teaching course topics and 50% working with student projects. He is very energetic and includes humor in his lectures. He mentioned you need to dedicate "HOURS AND HOURS" a week to the project to get a 100% on the weekly progress, but he only means 3-5 hours.

Professor Ricks took an interesting approach to the class, teaching it in sprints with self-graded progress reports, as in 2510 there is only one project that you develop as the semester continues. He gives great lectures and makes it clear he cares. Make sure you're invested in the class though, without motivation it can be easy to fall behind.

Dr. Ricks is the best for 3320. However, One project I typed xyzThing instead of xyz_thing. The test case failed because I changed my local test file to match my code. My mistake, but I never knew until he told me- way past due. He kept my grade at 50%- not because it was late, but because he assumed I copied the code from someone he had last year.

Dr. Ricks is a great professor. Class always felt engaging and challenging, and Ricks was great at helping with any struggles along the way. He's very kind and makes sure to connect with every student.
Had a great time. Ricks is an awesome professor, and he goes out of his way to help. Had a few assignments then a final project. Nothing to complain about.

Dr. Ricks is an outstanding professor, who expresses interest in individual students. If you have an issue with a project you're working on, he offers you help during your sprints. However, you need to put in the work outside of class if you want to present quality projects. Overall great professor, and a funny guy.

Dr. Ricks' sprint-based teaching is not for everyone. Most of the material in this course to justify the 4000-level status is what you learn by yourself in each sprint if you decide not to apply the bare minimum. At that point, you might as well self-study instead of spending money to take the class.

A great professor I've taken multiple times at this point. Course load is usually fairly light with you working on your own projects throughout the semester. YOU GRADE YOURSELF. Just show progress whenever he checks in with you, show up, and you'll be fine.

Dr Ricks is very helpful, caring and very good at teaching. He doesn't just read from slides but explains the concepts and has great animated powerpoint to help you understand how things work. Every assignment is explained so you will know what he wants. Mondays were sprint days and in class coding and 1 on 1 with him. Thrusdays were lectures.

He was accessible and helpful with homework questions. Lectures were good, and it was pretty easy if you did the work outside of class. I didn't have much experience with Python, but he made it easy to complete the assignments. I'm not a computer science whiz, but this was a fairly stress-free class. Start early on assignments and you'll be fine.
If you have question this teacher in not very helpful at.
Not very helpful, plus not reachable by email he has banker hours
Just not a good teacher

I loved the structure of this class - you basically get an A as long as you complete all of the assignments, which took a lot of pressure off for me and allowed me to actually spend time learning the content. He's a super funny guy who really cares about your success and understanding in the class - I'd take any class with him!

Really cares about the success of his students and takes time to make sure everyone understands what we're doing. One of the only CS professors I've had that understands tests are not a good way of teaching for Computer Science, and grades based on participation and your final project. GREAT professor and fun to listen to. Feel like I learned a lot

Ricks is honestly a really good guy, the class is sprint based meaning no traditional tests or homework. Everything is off of self chosen projects, as long as you take advantage of this and keep yourself somewhat busy the class will be a blast
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Graded by few things

review_text: Very knowledgeable and provides good feedback. This was a special topics course, but CSCI-4620 will be similar. Attendance isn't graded, but most of the material is delivered through in-class examples.  Thankfully, he does his best to keep class engaging. Source code is provided, but slides/notes are generally your own responsibility.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

review_text: Although Dr. Ricks was a new teacher, he was better than my previous tenured teachers. He truly cared about teaching his class. He had a genuine passion for his job. Be prepared to ask questions and go to the tutors. Although the class was non traditional, it was one of the best classes I have taken at UNO.

This review is over Rick's Spring Java 2 course. Overall I felt like a guinea pig and that's not a bad thing. We got to try many different learning styles for his first semester at UNO. If you want a teacher who is perky, cares about students, and really has some neat lessons that set him apart from PowerPoint professors this guy is the way to go.

Dr. Ricks was a little disorganized however it was his first time teaching Java 2. He changed the assignments a lot from the given criteria however it usually made them better and helped develop my knowledge of Java. The assignments were hard sometimes but it's not high school they should be hard. He really invested time in knowing his students.

Java 2. Not a bad instructor, just a non-traditional leture style, which he warns of at the beginning of the semester. If you talk to him, he is very understanding. His first semester was disorganized, but he will be an asset to UNO once settled in. Made my last programming class at UNO manageable.  Get lab help on the assignments & you'll be fine.

Intro to Computer Science 2. Dr. Ricks would preface about the lecture for the first 10 minutes of class and then jump right into coding. Ricks teaches this class differently than any other professor teaching the course. If you already know java, then you'll be fine. Dr. Ricks knows his stuff, but doesn't convey it to the students well.

She is honestly not that bad of a professor. I took her course last semester and she didn't assign us any pop quizzes or homework. It was mostly lecture notes and quizzes about the chapters. The class format can get a little messy sometimes with due dates being changed, either pushed back or dropped entirely. Class by itself was relatively easy.

A lot of these other reviews are simply... not my experience. No pop quizzes, no late policy, nothing. She did pretty much read straight from the textbook but her exams were all open book and pretty easy.

She often steals content from other people and universities. She is not knowledgeable about the courses she teaches. Furthermore, she knows nothing about IT nor how to use it. If you ask her any IT related questions, she will not be able to tell you the correct answer. I would recommend NOT taking her courses.

As noted by other reviews here she imports practically all of her materials from UC Berkeley which is fine, but she doesn't seem to have a good grasp of the knowledge herself and often struggles to explain the information. Even when I asked her questions outside of class for help, I rarely got a straight or helpful answer. Avoid if possible.
Used material from cs 188 of UC Berkely. Homework and programming assignments are all from cs 188 which are hard. Exams are easy and give bonus points.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: She honestly doesn't really care about students. She gives a lot of surprise quizzes on the material she teaches the same day. When she teaches something, she gives one example and expects you to know the rest by heart. Canvas modules where dis organized, she said we don't need book but then said we did. Reviews are easy, test are hard.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

review_text: She gives pop quizzes pretty much every class and they are HARD. Be prepared to teach yourself as she introduces topics on quizzes, and in lectures without fully explaining them.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

review_text: She provides classnotes that cover all the topics of quizes and exams. Discrete math is hard in nature, so you really need to go to the lectures and do your assignments. Then you'll be fine. She's very willing to help students after class.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Just avoid this teacher at all costs. Simply baffling teaching and testing style. She simply reads the notes almost verbatim out loud while drawing on the whiteboard and then immediately gives you a graded quiz over the information she just tried to teach. If it sounds ineffective it's because it is. Also has a weird "late to class" policy. Avoid.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: Very very tough teacher. Class material is tough in general, she did not make it easier. She has an accent that can be very hard to understand, and her handwriting is very terrible for notes. Don't expect to learn anything in class, you will have to use the internet to actual learn the material. Pop quizzes will destroy your grade. Dont recommend.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

review_text: Gives out pop quizzes almost weekly right after lessons before you can even study it, even on the first day of class, but can use notes. Accent and bad handwriting makes it hard to follow her lesson. Goes into more detail on subjects than other profs, avoid if not interested in subject. Nice though and has good office hours. Grade is mostly tests.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

review_text: One of the worst teachers I've had at UNO.  She doesn't seem to have a good grasp of the course material - she had trouble explaining many things and even admitted to having to Google some stuff to understand it herself!  Pop quizzes given at the end of most classes before we had time to really understand the material.  I would avoid this teacher!

Discrete Mathematics-boy, you're in for a surprise with this teacher. I can honestly say, that this is the worst teacher I have ever had. Ever. In my entire academic career.  It's not that I didn't do well in the course - I got an A, but that was just from reading the textbook. This teacher will not give you a clear explanation for anything.
Great teacher, always willing to help students.
Good instructor. Easy grader. Do the assignments and you will be fine. She made her class enjoyable.

Professor Lierler is a wonderful instructor! She is entertaining and keeps the class involved in a class that would otherwise be boring and tedious. She can relate to students (she herself is a recent grad) and understands that the subject can be difficult at times. Definitely recommend her.

She is a young teacher. I think she is knowledgeable, but is the worst teacher I've ever had. She changes her teaching methodology every couple of weeks. She focuses on things that are meaningless and totally misses the important material and concepts. Absolutely out of touch with students and what they need to succeed. Avoid at all cost!

rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

review_text: The professor is a nice person, but struggles with teaching. His lectures are hard to follow due to mumbling, and he doesn't engage with students during class. Dr. Song is a nice when I talked to him but other than that he is terrible at teaching. Most of the class is spent copying code from his slides and changing it for whatever assignment you need it for. The 2 exams are 60% of your grade and are both extremely difficult and oddly fair as he seems to curve them. That being said his grading isn't clear

Hands down the worse professor ever. Material you will learn in class is absolutely useless and obsolete. Very hard to understand when he lecture. The course material and project are helpful and give you handful skills. Homework is not that hard even you don't have much experience on Java. Attendance is required otherwise you will not get points.

Dr. Song is great. His class is not hard but you have to do homework carefully. There is a group project in this class and you will be fine as long as you follow the course schedule. The tech stack of project is based on what you learned in the lecture. Btw he is a fairly lenient grader.
Overall the course material is useless and half of the semester we learned by ourselves by reading papers, he didn't teach anything
rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Group projects

review_text: Lectures and projects feature very useful practical skills that you can bring up in a job interview. There was often in-class work that you had to come to class to do - if you didn't come to class, you didn't get the credit. The in-class work was also good practice that reinforced the practical skills we would be using for the group project.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

review_text: If you pay attention in this class, you can pick up some solid and useful coding skills. By the end, you'll have a couple web app servlets, backed up by SQL databases, running on an Amazon Linux server that can be accessed from anywhere on the web. This framework allows you to implement almost any web apps you can think of. Save the slides.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Poor English, useless materials and teaching.

Dr. Song is by far the worst professor I've had at UNO. He just reads right off the slides that he downloaded for the book and never explains any of the concepts that are discussed in any meaningful detail. His slides use out of date examples that won't work on newer machines, so Dr. Song has to find weird hacks to make his examples work. Horrible.

Dr. Song makes me realize how much UNO really cares about their students. They care enough to hire someone who can barely speak English and is hands down the worst professor I've EVER had! Ez A , but you won't learn anything

Dr. Song didn't seem to care about this class at all.   In no particular order; he frequently cut off class early and walked out without answering any questions, he didn't respond to a single email I sent him, assignments due in February weren't graded until late April, he was very soft spoken and didn't engage students at all.

Taking his class was so boring. It seems like he is knowledgable about the subject BUT he goes over the material super quick and doesn't pause to check. He doesn't release lectures before the class so it's hard to follow along. lets you use cheat sheets on test, for this reason I hardly paid attention to lectures and passed by using cheat sheets.

Song is one of the most disorganized professors I've ever had.  He posts assignments and announcements in random places with no rhyme or reason.  He has several PPT lectures for every topic. 3 exams, lot of homeworks. You will struggle to understand her as she can't speak proper english and be articulate. How she became a teacher is a mystery. Had Artificial Intelligence CS4450/8456 with her. She has a different accent. She just took all the material of UC BERKLEY AI COURSE. Wish her teaching was also the same level. Avoid if possible
Never understood a single concept. Exams and Assignments are so hard which she never discussed in class.
CS 4450/8456 Teaching is not upto the level which is enough for her assignments and Exams.

She is willing to help students, but she has a pretty big disconnect from students and often doesn't understand what they are asking her. Her tests are also really hard and ask questions about things we haven't even talked about in class. Avoid if you can.

She has an accent and it is very difficult to understand her. You will barely understand her lectures, so get ready to read by yourself. She should consider easy going as her lectures are boring and very hard to understand. Skip if you can.

review_text: Really hard to understand the material, overall, needs to learn lots of material by myself

rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Group projects

review_text: It's advised to take his class online. Exams are held both online and offline, posing challenges in ensuring fairness. Moreover, the assignments are typically demanding, requiring a strong foundation in JAVA programming. To achieve good grades in exams, it's recommended to watch the ZOOM recordings 2-3 times and take notes.

I agree he could be more organized but everything he says in lecture is what you will need for exams. I highly recommend re-watching lectures. Also, if you really want to do well, just read the book. Be prepared. Especially more-so for 2125. Having taken other cs courses in other schools I can tell you he's one of the good ones.

Great lecturer but not very organized. Two of the four homework were changed the day before they were due and a week after I had submitted them. Tests are few and short and getting one question incorrect can result in a bad grade. More than 50% of my grades were not returned till the day grades were due. Nice guy, good lecturer, bad administrator.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

review_text: Read the textbook/watch youtube videos bc you'll be tested thoroughly on topics covered. Go through the code he writes and practice doing it yourself, pay attention in class, and do your hw and you'll do well enough to pass. You need to put in way more effort than that for an A, and this is coming from someone who breezed through Java I&II w/ an A.

Dr. Summa knows what he's talking about, problem is some students don't. His homework is hard so make sure to get help. He'll only help you so much even if you need it further broken down. Study for test. This class is not an easy A, unless you know what you're doing. Overall he's not bad but you'll definitely work for a good grade.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: Wow! Great Professor. I usually don't rate professors but I had to make an exception for Dr. Summa. He makes sure his students understand the material, always pauses to ask students if they have any questions. Although he does speak in paragraphs rather than sentences, it's easy to understand the material from him. He has a good sense of humor too!

Oustanding Professor, adjust your schedule to take his classes: Java 1, 2 and Data Structures. You will come out understanding everything, top-notch teacher. Beyond that he will work with you on any personal issues you are having (stay in touch), always responds to emails in a friendly and helpful way, and the bonus point options are plentiful.

Dr. Summa is decent professor. For every class he reads straight from the powerpoint and some classes create a code in front of you. He's a good teacher for those who can pick up things easily. I did good in his class, there were a few things i completely understood and other classes I'm completely lost. If you're a visual learner you'll be fine
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

review_text: I would say dr. Summa is a good teacher, he gives good lectures. As long as you show up to class and study for tests it will be an easy A.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: I already love computer science but taking him made me like it that much more.

Summa is a great professor, he explains things well. I came into computer science not knowing ANYTHING about coding and what not and having Summa made me more confident in my knowledge of the subject. Be sure to pay attention to his lectures and you can always ask questions. Don't slack off or you won't do as well!

Summa is awesome. His lectures are in depth and clear, with the subject matter he chooses being interesting enough to keep you interested (i.e. no boring bank account example programs with this guy). He will type out entire programs as examples to concepts he goes over in class and ensures no one leaves confused, so long as they're willing to ask.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

One of the best teacher so far in my CSCI classes. He lectures with power point(i know get boring at times), but he goes over key concepts that you will need to learn. rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: This is the guy for any CSCI major. Lectures are good, homeworks are challenging and his grading system benefits you. I guarantee you will learn valuable skills in this class. Summa is a really nice guy and will thoroughly explain things if you are lost
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: He uses slides to guide his lectures, which are posted on Moodle. I felt this was ok, since we talk about a lot of concepts for more sophisticated programming. rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: He is one of the best UNO has to offer. The classes are hard work but Dr. Summa will work with you.

Dr. Summa is a great teacher who explains everything clearly and encourages students to ask questions. He posts the power points he uses online so you can reference them on your own. The class is difficult and you will have to study but he is always available to help. The homework is the hardest part, but he gives lots of time for each assignment.

Class is not easy if you have no prior programming experience, but Chris does a good job explaining everything. Don't get frustrated. The HW problems are far from intuitive, but he give a lot of time to complete each assignment. Ask questions. Recommended.

I have to say that after taking Dr.Suma, I have come to understand that he is one of the nicest guys you will ever meet,Im not saying his class will be easy, it was for me, you will need to study, His test are hard, but if you study you will be fine, his Home work assignment was fun to write, all in all i really enjoyed his class,highly recommended
Very good teacher. He is very concerned with a students progress and is more than willing to help you should you need it. Course material is to the point and on topic. I would highly recommend him.
Ridiculously unclear in class, on homework prompt, and test questions and if you ask him a question, regardless of "is this a or b," his answer will be incomprehensible.

The professor as a person is very sweet, but as a professor he's not great at all. review_text: Boring, monotoned lectures off of useful PowerPoints. Long, but sparse, homework assignments, which he didn't grade until the end of the semester (20% of grade). Hes a great professor. The lectures consist of PowerPoint slides that are very clear. For the tests, he provides old tests to study from that are very similar to the tests he gives out. Overall, very clear and you will actually learn a lot about algorithm analysis.
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: The worst class I have ever taken. I didn't learn anything in this class. Boring lecture, lengthy tests, last minute homework. And never got back the homework grade in the whole semester.

review_text: He is very amicable with students.

Dr. Depano is ALWAYS available to help anyone who needs it. If you have real life problems that are causing you difficulty, he understands and will be fair and accommodate your situation within reason to make sure you don't drop the course.

Excellent instructor! Extremely helpful and prompt response to emails!
he is awesome. easy grader. but his classes are boring. i usually taught myself by reading the book.

I love this man. Simply I love him. God bless his ability to teach because he taught in a way which is super hard for me to forget the material. I took networking and telecom  with him — he's a straight up GOAT.

He a boss

Dr. Nur is a perfect example of a great professor. Although lectures may be difficult for some, he will always be there to help if you ask for it. Do not be afraid to reach out to him, he is on your side.. As a professor and as a normal human being!! Love this guy.

Professor Nur is very caring and helpful. His tests are very easy and come straight from his exam review. Little programming is required. Attend classes for the pop quizzes as it contributes a lot to the final grade. Its an easy A class with professor Nur. Would like to take him again.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
